articles.title: ESIC Invites Applications for Admission to MBBS, BDS and B.Sc. Nursing Courses under Ward of Insured Person Quota for Academic Session 2026-27
articles.full_content:
783 Seats Allocated Across 22 Medical Colleges Pan-India to Empower Children of ESI Covered Workers Online Portal For Ward Of IP Certificate Closes on 21st June, 2026
The Employees’ State Insurance Corporation (ESIC), under the Ministry of Labour & Employment, Government of India, has invited applications for admission to undergraduate medical and nursing courses for the academic session 2026-27 under the Ward of Insured Person (Ward of IP) quota. The admission opportunity is available exclusively for the dependent children of workers covered under the Employees’ State Insurance (ESI) Scheme and reflects ESIC’s continued commitment towards extending meaningful social security and educational benefits to the families of insured workers across the country.
A total of 783 seats have been provisionally earmarked under the Ward of IP quota for the academic session 2026-27, comprising 695 MBBS seats, 28 BDS seats and 60 B.Sc. Nursing seats across 22 medical, dental and nursing institutions. These include ESIC Medical Colleges located in various States and select State Government Medical Colleges where seats have been reserved for eligible Wards of Insured Persons. The admissions will be conducted through the centralized counselling process administered by the Directorate General of Health Services (DGHS), Ministry of Health and Family Welfare.
The Ward of IP quota is one of the flagship welfare initiatives of ESIC aimed at supporting the educational aspirations of workers’ families. The scheme enables eligible students to pursue professional medical and nursing education at highly subsidized tuition fees. Students admitted under the quota are required to pay an annual tuition fee of only ₹24,000 for MBBS and BDS courses and ₹10,000 for B.Sc. Nursing courses, making quality medical education accessible and affordable for beneficiaries of the ESI Scheme. No capitation fee or donation is permitted for admission under this quota.
To be eligible, the parent must be a registered Insured Person under the ESI Act and should have been an active contributor to the ESI Scheme as on 30 September 2025. The applicant must be a dependent child of the Insured Person, should have appeared in NEET (UG) 2026 and must satisfy the eligibility requirements prescribed by the National Medical Commission, Dental Council of India, Indian Nursing Council, National Testing Agency and Medical Counselling Committee. Male candidates should not be above 21 years of age as on 08 March 2026, while the age restriction is not applicable to dependent unmarried female Wards of Insured Persons in accordance with the provisions of the ESI Act, 1948.
Eligible candidates are required to obtain a valid Ward of IP Certificate issued by ESIC, which is mandatory for participation in the admission process. articles.title: Union Environment Minister inaugurates 18 Namo Oxygen Parks in Delhi; Union and Delhi Governments launch major Green Initiatives on World Environment Day 2026
articles.full_content:
Shri Bhupender Yadav highlights India's Global Environmental Leadership; Calls for Community-Led Ecological Conservation Delhi to plant 15 Lakh Trees this Year; Chief Minister stresses Public Participation in Nurturing Green Cover Working Plan for NCT Delhi, Asola Bhatti Sanctuary Management Plan and Delhi Bird Atlas released to strengthen Scientific Environmental Governance
The Union Minister for Environment, Forest and Climate Change, Shri Bhupender Yadav, along with the Chief Minister of Delhi, Smt. Rekha Gupta, today inaugurated eighteen ‘Namo Oxygen Parks’ across Delhi and launched a series of environmental initiatives under the nationwide campaign ‘Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam’ on the occasion of World Environment Day 2026. The event, held at Namo Oxygen Park, Maidangarhi, marked a significant step towards strengthening urban greening, improving air quality and promoting community-led environmental stewardship in the National Capital Territory.
Other dignitaries gracing the occasion included Union Minister of State (EFCC), Shri Kirti Vardhan Singh; Minister for Environment, Forest and Wildlife (NCT Delhi), Sardar Manjinder Singh Sirsa; and other senior officials from Union Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change, Delhi Government, among others.
Addressing the gathering, Shri Bhupender Yadav said that humanity today faces three major environmental challenges — climate change, biodiversity loss and land degradation leading to desertification. He noted that under the leadership of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi, India has emerged as a global leader in environmental action, achieving major milestones including becoming one of the world's leading nations in solar capacity addition, implementing the PM Surya Ghar Yojana, establishing the International Solar Alliance, launching the International Big Cat Alliance (IBCA), expanding Ramsar Wetland sites and achieving several Nationally Determined Contribution (NDC) targets ahead of schedule under the UN Framework Convention on Climate Change.
The Minister emphasized that environmental conservation requires collective action and active public participation. Referring to the Prime Minister's vision of ‘Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam’, he said the campaign has transformed tree plantation into a people's movement rooted in gratitude, responsibility and environmental consciousness. He urged citizens not only to plant trees but also to nurture and protect them for future generations.
Highlighting efforts to improve Delhi's environment, Shri Yadav stated that the Delhi Government, in coordination with the Commission for Air Quality Management (CAQM), is taking active measures to address major sources of air pollution including road dust, vehicular emissions and industrial pollution. These measures include installation of Online Continuous Emission Monitoring Systems (OCEMS), deployment of Air Pollution Control Devices (APCDs), mechanized deep cleaning of roads and accelerated transition towards electric mobility.

In a major reform, Government announces measures to deepen G-Sec market and facilitate greater Foreign Portfolio Investment (FPI) in equity segment
articles.full_content:
In line with the Government's commitment to strengthen India's position as a leading global investment destination and to deepen the capital market, the Ministry of Finance has taken a series of measures aimed at enhancing the ease of investment for individual Persons Resident Outside India (PROIs) and Foreign Portfolio Investors (FPIs), and to attract stable long-term foreign capital flows.
Building on the recent initiatives to enhance ease of doing business in capital markets, the Government has undertaken further reforms to make foreign investment in equities and G-Secs more accessible, efficient, and globally competitive.
Liberalisation of investment by individual Persons Resident Outside India under the Foreign Exchange Management (Non- Debt Instrument) Rules, 2019
The Union Minister for Finance and Corporate Affairs announced in the Union Budget FY2026-27 that individual Persons Resident Outside India (PROI) will be permitted to invest in equity instruments of listed Indian companies through the Portfolio Investment Scheme which was hitherto available only to NRIs/OCIs; and the investment limit will be increased for an individual PROI under this scheme from 5% to 10% in any company, with an overall investment limit for all individual PROIs to 24%, from the current 10%.
To implement the same, Department of Economic Affairs (DEA) is notifying the Foreign Exchange Management (Non-Debt Instruments) (Third Amendment) Rules, 2026.
This notification will facilitate a more proactive mobilisation of foreign portfolio capital by leveraging the existing onboarding systems already in place for NRI/OCI investors. Simplified onboarding and reduced compliance requirements would further enhance ease of doing business, while attracting a broader base of relatively stable individual foreign investors. This will also support greater and more stable foreign inflows into Indian equity markets.
Review of the regulatory framework for FPI investment in Government securities
With the view to enhance participation by FPIs in G-Sec, the Government has decided to expand the list of specified securities under the Fully Accessible Route (FAR) to also include new issuances in Government securities in tenors of 15, 30 and 40 years as also Sovereign Green Bonds (SGrBs) in the tenors of FAR-eligible securities. Further, with respect to FPI investments under General Route, it has been decided to remove the three restrictions, viz. short-term investment limit, concentration limit and the security-wise limit for investments by Foreign Portfolio Investors (FPIs) in Government securities, while retaining the overall quantitative investment limit of 6 per cent of the outstanding stock of the Central Government securities and 2 per cent of the State Government securities (SGSs). The sub-categories of investment limits, viz., 'general' and 'long-term' will also be merged into a single limit for investment in Government securities and SGSs, respectively.

articles.title: Shri Hardeep Singh Puri holds talks with Venezuela's Acting President; Reaffirms Venezuela's importance in India's energy diversification strategy
India’s Biodiversity: Commitments and Achievements
articles.full_content:
From legal frameworks to local action
India’s biodiversity governance is organised through a three‑tier structure that links national policy with state action and local implementation. The National Biodiversity Authority operates at the national level, while State Biodiversity Boards and Union Territory Biodiversity Councils guide action at the state and provincial levels. At the local level, Biodiversity Management Committees in rural and urban bodies prepare People’s Biodiversity Registers and support community‑led conservation. India has established more than 2,76,653 BMCs and over 2,72,648 People’s Biodiversity Registers, documenting local species, ecosystems and traditional knowledge across the country. Together, these institutions and processes strengthen ecological resilience, local stewardship and alignment with global biodiversity goals under the Convention on Biological Diversity (CBD).
Conserving Natural Capital for a Resilient Future
Biodiversity is central to India’s environmental and development priorities. It supports food security, livelihoods, climate resilience, and ecological balance. India’s forests, wetlands, mountains, coasts, deserts, grasslands, and marine ecosystems sustain diverse species and communities. Millions of local communities depend on these natural resources for daily life. India continues to consrerve its natural capital and biodiversity commitments through policies, institutions, and community participation aligned with global frameworks. Through these efforts, India contributes to global biodiversity goals while strengthening conservation at the national and local levels. India’s approach emphasises local stewardship, recognising that community-led action is essential for achieving broader biodiversity outcomes.
Over the past decade, India has adopted an integrated approach to biodiversity conservation that combines scientific management, habitat restoration, species recovery programmes, and community participation. Significant efforts have been made to expand protected areas, strengthen wildlife monitoring, restore degraded ecosystems, and promote sustainable use of biological resources. These initiatives have enhanced ecological resilience, supported local livelihoods, and reinforced India's position as a global leader in biodiversity conservation. By aligning conservation goals with sustainable development objectives, India is working towards securing its natural heritage for future generations while fostering inclusive and environmentally responsible growth.
Understanding Biodiversity and Its Importance
Biodiversity refers to the variety of life on earth, including plants, animals, microorganisms and the ecosystems they form. It underpins ecological balance and supports key ecosystem services such as pollination, soil formation, nutrient cycling, water purification and climate regulation. Biodiversity sustains the natural systems that make life possible and productive across regions.

articles.title: Prime Minister wishes everyone on World Environment Day
articles.full_content:
PM shares a Sanskrit Subhashitam emphasizing nature's conservation
Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi today extended his best wishes to everyone on World Environment Day, applauding all those passionate about environmental conservation.
The Prime Minister remarked that this day serves to reaffirm the commitment to protecting the environment and furthering sustainable growth, highlighting the numerous government initiatives over the last decade that have expanded green cover and increased the population of several animal species.
Shri Modi noted that the people of India have demonstrated how collective efforts, robust policies, belief in science, and innovation can significantly improve the environment.
The Prime Minister observed that India takes immense pride in its biological diversity, which supports countless species and livelihoods through diverse ecosystems.
Highlighting noteworthy strides in special species recovery, Shri Modi pointed out that conservation efforts for the Great Indian Bustard, snow leopards, sloth bears, and Cheetahs offer a glimpse of how sustained commitment can help restore wildlife and ecosystems.
The Prime Minister added that initiatives like ‘Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam’ have made significant contributions towards adding nearly 1.1 lakh hectares of forest every year.
Shri Modi affirmed that guided by the principle of ‘One Earth, One Family and One Future’, India will continue working towards a cleaner, greener, and more sustainable planet through the spirit of Mission LiFE.
The Prime Minister emphasized that this deep cultural ethos is beautifully reflected in traditional Indian wisdom, sharing a sacred Sanskrit Subhashitam to reinforce the message of environmental stewardship.
In a series of posts on X, the Prime Minister shared:
"Best wishes to everyone on World Environment Day. I would like to applaud all those passionate about environmental conservation. This is a day to reaffirm our commitment to protecting our environment and furthering growth that is sustainable. Numerous efforts by our Government over the last decade highlight our work in this direction. Some of India’s key successes include expanding green cover and a rise in the population of several animals. The people of India have shown how collective efforts, policies, belief in science and innovation can improve our environment.”

articles.title: PROVISIONAL ESTIMATES OF ANNUAL GROSS DOMESTIC PRODUCT FOR 2025-26 AND QUARTERLY ESTIMATES OF GROSS DOMESTIC PRODUCT FOR THE FOURTH QUARTER (JANUARY-MARCH) OF 2025-26
articles.full_content:
Provisional Estimates for the Financial Year 2025-26 shows Real GDP Growth Rate of 7.7% In Q4 (January-March) of FY 2025-26, Real GDP has been estimated to grow by 7.8%
The Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI) is releasing in this Press Note, the Provisional Estimates (PE) of Annual Gross Domestic Product (GDP) for the Financial Year (2025-26) and Quarterly Estimates of GDP for the Fourth Quarter (Q4, January-March) of FY 2025-26 along with its Expenditure components both at Constant (2022-23) and Current prices. The Ministry has released the New Series of Annual and Quarterly GDP estimates with base year 2022-23 on 27 th February, 2026 . In that release, the Quarterly GDP estimates and other aggregates were published from Q1 (April-June) of 2022-23 to Q3 (October-December) of FY 2025-26. Consequently, the Second Advance Estimates released on February, 2026 was based on information available for the FY 2025-26 upto Q3. Now, on account of information received for the Q4 of FY 2025-26, the Annual Estimates for the FY 2025-26 have been revised and being released in this press note. The press release is structured as follows.
Annual GDP Estimates and Growth Rates
Quarterly GDP Estimates and Growth Rates
Methodology and Major Data Sources
• Provisional Estimates of GDP along with Expenditure Components and National Income at Constant and Current Prices (Table 1 and 2)
• Provisional Estimates of Gross Value Added (GVA) at Constant and Current Prices (Table 3 & 4)
• Quarterly GDP Estimates along with Expenditure Components at Constant and Current Prices (Table 5, 6, 7 & 8)
• Year-on-Year (Y-o-Y) Growth Rates in Indicators
I. Annual GDP Estimates and Growth Rates
Real GDP or GDP at Constant Prices is estimated to attain a level of ₹ 323.12 lakh crore in the FY 2025-26 , against the First Revised Estimate (FRE) of GDP for the year 2024-25 of ₹ 299.89 lakh crore . The growth rate in Real GDP during 2025-26 is estimated at 7.7% as compared to 7.1 % in 2024-25 . Nominal GDP or GDP at Current Prices is estimated to attain a level of ₹ 346.36 lakh crore in the year 2025-26, against ₹ 318.07 lakh crore in 2024-25, showing a growth rate of 8.9% .
Real GVA is estimated at ₹ 294.91 lakh crore in the year 2025-26 , against ₹ 273.36 lakh crore in FY 2024-25 , registering a growth rate of 7.9% as compared to 7.3% growth rate in 2024-25 . Nominal GVA is estimated to attain a level of ₹ 314.87 lakh crore during FY 2025-26, against ₹ 288.54 lakh crore in 2024-25, showing a growth rate of 9.1%.

articles.title: 100 Years of solar data traces new clues on how the Sun’s surface tracks its 11-Year Activity Cycle
articles.full_content:
The Kodaikanal solar observatory, known for the oldest continuous series of solar data collected in India, have helped uncover how giant convection patterns on the Sun respond to solar activity, providing insights for future solar cycle prediction.
Much like a boiling pot of water on a stove, the energy generated within the Sun is transported through its outer layers via convection. Convective cells lead to the formation of small-scale granulations and large scale supergranulations as a network structure on the solar surface.
The network cells have an average lifetime of 24 hr, and a size of about 30,000 km., The width of the cooler intergranular lanes is about 6000 km. What is the origin of these supergranulations, what determines their size, and what is their relation with the 11-year solar cycle continue to be a puzzle. A recent study from the Indian Institute of Astrophysics based on more than 100 years of data from the Kodaikanal Solar Observatory sheds some light on these questions.
This observed network arises due to magnetic flux concentration at cell boundaries as a consequence of supergranular convection. Skylab observations in the 1970s have shown that the chromospheric network extends to the transition region as the Extreme UltraViolet (EUV) network. The network is dominant in the midtransition region, and it disintegrates in the corona.
Scientists from Indian Institute of Astrophysics (IIA), an autonomous institute of the Department of Science and Technology (DST) examined the relationship between the solar cycle and two physical quantities like lane widths and intensities.
Fig : Ca II K spectroheliograms from Kodaikanal Solar Observatory. The image on the left is taken during the solar minimum year of 1913, and the one on the right is taken during the solar maximum year of 1917.
To see how the time lag between the quantities varies in different latitudes, the researchers led by Prof KP Raju examined the cross-correlation between these quantities to see where the correlation coefficient reaches a maximum.
With Lane widths and intensities at different latitudes from the Ca II K spectroheliograms from the 100-year Kodaikanal archival data from 1907, the study published in the Astrophysical Journal Letters, analyzed 34,000 Ca II K images and found that lane widths and intensities correlate strongly with sunspot numbers, peaking around +/-(11–22)° latitude. The peak correlation for lane widths occurs at (18 +/- 2)°N and (20 +/- 2)°S, while for intensities, it is at (13 +/- 2)°N and (14 +/- 2)°S, indicating no unique latitude follows the solar cycle for all quantities. Lane width correlations peak during solar maximum, whereas intensity correlations peak 1.25–1.5 years after solar maximum, suggesting a time lag in their responses to solar activity. The study finds that the lag varies with latitude: zero near ±20°, decreasing towards higher latitudes, and increasing towards the equator. For lane widths, the lag ranges from 0.5 to 0.8 years

Much like a boiling pot of water on a stove, the energy generated within the Sun is transported through its outer layers via convection. Convective cells lead to the formation of small-scale granulations and large scale supergranulations as a network structure on the solar surface.

articles.title: TDB-DST supports M/s Greenjoules Private Limited, Pune for Commercialization of Indigenous Second-Generation Diesel Equivalent Biofuel Technology
articles.full_content:
As India accelerates its transition towards cleaner energy sources and circular economy-driven industrial growth, the Technology Development Board (TDB), Department of Science & Technology (DST), Government of India, has extended support to M/s Greenjoules Private Limited, Pune for its project titled “Manufacturing of 2nd Generation Diesel Equivalent Biofuel.” The project aims to establish a commercial-scale facility for the production of advanced biofuels derived from agricultural waste and agro-processing residues, contributing to India's energy security, waste-to-wealth mission, and net-zero aspirations.
The proposed project involves setting up a greenfield manufacturing facility for the production of Abhilasha Biofuels (ABF)—a next-generation renewable diesel and naphtha alternative developed entirely through indigenous technology. Designed as a "drop-in" fuel, ABF can directly replace conventional fossil-based diesel without requiring any modifications to existing engines, fuel systems, or distribution infrastructure, making it a practical and scalable solution for decarbonizing transportation and industrial energy consumption.
Developed entirely in India, the technology integrates innovative thermo-chemical conversion processes that transform agricultural residues and agro-industrial waste into high-quality renewable fuels. The process combines thermal cracking, catalytic upgrading, and downstream refining technologies to produce biofuels that meet established quality specifications comparable to their fossil-derived counterparts. The fuel has already been successfully produced from nearly forty different feedstocks, demonstrating remarkable flexibility and adaptability to India's diverse biomass resources.
A distinctive feature of the technology lies in Greenjoules' proprietary expertise in identifying optimal biomass blends and matching them with customized catalyst systems to maximize fuel yields and production efficiency. While individual process steps are known industrial practices, the company's unique integration of feedstock selection, catalyst optimization, and process engineering constitutes its core intellectual property and enables commercially viable production of advanced biofuels from low-value agricultural waste.
The project will further enhance the company's capabilities in producing renewable diesel, renewable naphtha, biochar, and gaseous fuels, creating a comprehensive waste-to-energy ecosystem. By converting agricultural residues that are often burned or discarded into valuable energy products, the technology addresses multiple national priorities simultaneously, including reduction of stubble burning, promotion of clean energy, strengthening of rural value chains, and lowering dependence on imported fossil fuels.
The initiative aligns closely with the Government of India's National Biofuel Policy, circular economy objectives, and broader efforts to promote sustainable industrial development through indigenous technolog

articles.title: Rashtriya Ayurveda Vidyapeeth Observes World Environment Day 2026 with Plantation Drive and Sapling Distribution
articles.full_content:
Rashtriya Ayurveda Vidyapeeth (RAV), New Delhi, celebrated World Environment Day 2026 through a plantation drive and sapling distribution programme, reaffirming its commitment to environmental conservation and sustainable living. The event was organized in line with this year's World Environment Day theme, "Inspired by Nature. For Climate. For Our Future."
The programme was attended by Vaidya Devendra Triguna, President, Governing Body, RAV; Dr. Vandana Siroha, Director, Rashtriya Ayurveda Vidyapeeth; Dr. Sushrut Kanaujia, President, Board of Ethics and Registration, National Commission for Indian System of Medicine (NCISM); and Dr. B.L. Mehra, Former Member, Medical Assessment and Rating Board, NCISM.
As part of the celebration, faculty members, officers, staff, and students participated in a plantation drive on the Vidyapeeth campus, reaffirming their commitment to environmental conservation and ecological sustainability. The initiative aimed to promote environmental awareness and encourage collective action towards increasing green cover and environmental stewardship. The event also highlighted the importance of conserving medicinal plants, which form the foundation of Ayurvedic healthcare and India's rich traditional knowledge heritage.
The dignitaries highlighted Ayurveda's ecological philosophy, which emphasizes the interconnectedness of human beings and nature. Referring to the concepts of Panchamahabhuta Siddhanta and Loka-Purusha Samya, they noted that environmental balance is fundamental to human health and well-being. They further observed that Ayurvedic principles encourage sustainable lifestyles, mindful consumption, and responsible use of natural resources, reinforcing the relevance of traditional knowledge systems in addressing contemporary environmental challenges.
A special feature of the programme was the distribution of saplings among officials and staff members of the Vidyapeeth. The initiative aimed to encourage participants to actively contribute to environmental conservation by planting and nurturing trees in their communities.
Participants reaffirmed their commitment to sustainable living, while the event underscored the continued relevance of Ayurveda in promoting environmental conservation, sustainable development, and harmony with nature.
articles.summary: Rashtriya Ayurveda Vidyapeeth (RAV), New Delhi, celebrated World Environment Day 2026 through a plantation drive and sapling distribution programme, reaffirming its commitment to environmental conservation and sustainable living. The event was organized in line with this year's World Environment Day theme, "Inspired by Nature. For Our Future." They encouraged students to become responsible environmental stewards and contribute towards building a cleaner, greener, and more sustainable future.
As part of the celebrations, competitions including Poster Making, Slogan Writing, and Pot Decorating were organized to promote environmental awareness and creativity among students. articles.title: Department of Posts Releases Two Special Covers on World Environment Day 2026 in Kolkata
articles.full_content:
Special Covers Highlight Plastic Pollution Awareness and Eco-Friendly Traditional Craft of West Bengal
Kolkata, 6th June 2026
The Department of Posts, West Bengal Circle, released two Special Covers on the occasion of World Environment Day 2026 at Kolkata GPO, Kolkata, on 5thJune 2026. The Special Covers commemorate the themes “Ending Plastic Pollution – the Key to a Cleaner Planet” and “Sitalpati – Handwoven Eco-friendly Mat with its Natural Cooling Properties”.
The Special Covers were released by Shri Ashok Kumar, Chief Postmaster General, West Bengal Circle, in the presence of Shri Riju Ganguly, Postmaster General, South Bengal Region and Mails & Business Development, along with distinguished guests, philatelists and postal officials.
The Special Cover on “Ending Plastic Pollution” underscores the urgent need for collective action to reduce plastic waste and promote sustainable alternatives for a cleaner and healthier environment. Through philately, the Department seeks to spread awareness about environmental conservation and encourage responsible practices that contribute to a sustainable future.
The Special Cover on “Sitalpati” showcases the traditional handwoven craft of Cooch Behar, West Bengal. Renowned for its natural cooling properties, Sitalpati represents an eco-friendly and sustainable craft tradition that has been preserved and passed down through generations. The Special Cover highlights the cultural significance of this indigenous handicraft and its relevance in promoting environmentally sustainable lifestyles.
The release of these Special Covers on World Environment Day reinforces the Department’s efforts to use philately as an effective platform for disseminating messages on sustainability, environmental stewardship and cultural preservation.

articles.title: BSF CONDUCTS EXTENSIVE TREE PLANTATION AT VARIOUS BORDER POSTS AND HEADQUARTERS ON THE OCCASION OF WORLD ENVIRONMENT DAY
articles.full_content:
On the auspicious occasion of World Environment Day, observed annually on June 5th, BSF organized a widespread tree plantation campaign across its various border outposts and HQrs. The theme for this year's World Environment Day is 'Beat Plastic Pollution, Ecosystem Restoration' which aligns perfectly with BSF's vision of a greener and healthier planet.
In its unwavering commitment towards environmental conservation, this initiative aimed to reinforce BSF's dedication to sustainability and create a positive impact on the environment.
By actively participating in this endeavor, IG BSF Gujarat and All Ranks contributed towards restoration of ecological balance, mitigating climate change and enhancing the natural beauty of the surrounding areas.
The drive involved planting of a diverse range of tree species, including both indigenous and exotic varieties, chosen specifically to suit the local ecosystems. These trees will not only help combat air pollution but also provide habitat for wildlife, conserve soil and beautify the surroundings.
Speaking about the initiative, Sh. Abhishek Pathak, IG BSF Gujarat Frontier, stated, "We are thrilled to celebrate World Environment Day with a significant tree plantation drive. BSF is firmly committed to environmental sustainability and understands the critical role of trees in combatting climate change. Through this campaign, we aim to create a positive impact on the environment, enhance biodiversity and promote a healthier ecosystem along the border regions."
BSF in close coordination with local communities, educational institutions and environmental organizations has been organising similar tree plantation drive throughout the year.
On 05th June 2026, a tree plantation program was organized on the occasion of World Environment Day under the aegis of BWWA (BSF Wives Welfare Association). Saplings of different species of fruit bearing and exotic trees were planted in the premises of Ankur Play School by BWWA members of BSF campus Gandhinagar.
On the occasion BWWA members were urged upon to conserve the environment by tree plantation and to follow this year’s theme of ‘Beat Plastic pollution, Ecosystem Restoration’ by limiting use of plastic in daily life to prevent damages to environment.

For Climate. For Our Future."
The programme was presided over by Dr. Engam Pame, IIS, Regional Director, CBC & PIB Imphal . Other dignitaries present on the dais included Shri H. Deepakkumar Sharma, FEO , Dr. N. Delivering the welcome address, Shri H. Deepakkumar Sharma welcomed the gathering and appealed to everyone to adopt eco-friendly products and practices at the individual, family, and community levels. He emphasized that environmental protection begins with small actions taken by every citizen.
In her keynote address, Dr. N. Reena Devi highlighted the traditional methods of environmental and ecosystem conservation practiced by communities over generations. She remarked that while people are generally aware of the importance of protecting the environment, the real challenge lies in translating awareness into action. She urged everyone to make a personal commitment to adopt practical and sustainable environmental practices for the benefit of future generations.
In his presidential address, Dr. Engam Pame underscored the significance of observing World Environment Day and elaborated on this year's theme. He reflected on the noticeable climatic changes witnessed from the 1980s and 1990s to the present day and stressed the urgent need to address environmental degradation. He called upon citizens to respect Mother Earth and nature, emphasizing that such observances should not be merely symbolic but should inspire meaningful action.
Dr. Pame also highlighted the close relationship between a healthy environment and human well-being, stating that without a clean and healthy environment, good health and quality of life cannot be sustained. Referring to the growing threat of various forms of pollution, he urged collective commitment to combating pollution through increased tree plantation, environmental stewardship, and the use of biodegradable materials while minimizing plastic consumption.

Indian Railways Celebrates World Environment Day 2026, Reaffirming Commitment to Sustainability, Environmental Responsibility and a Cleaner, Greener Future
articles.full_content:
Chairman & CEO, Railway Board Launches Solid Waste Management Rules, 2026 SOP Booklet to Strengthen Waste Management and Environmental Compliance Across Indian Railways North-Eastern Railway Secures First Position in Swachhata Pakhwada Awards; North-Western Railway and Southern Railway Follow
Indian Railways marked World Environment Day 2026 today with a focused programme at the Ministry of Railways, reaffirming its commitment towards environmental sustainability, cleanliness and responsible resource management across the railway network.
A major highlight of the programme was the launch of the Solid Waste Management Rules, 2026 - Standard Operating Procedures (SOP) Booklet by the Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, Railway Board, Shri Satish Kumar . The booklet has been developed to streamline waste management practices, strengthen environmental compliance and promote uniform implementation of sustainable waste management systems across Indian Railways.
Addressing the gathering, the Chairman and CEO, Railway Board emphasized the importance of integrating sustainability into day-to-day railway operations and fostering a culture of cleanliness, environmental responsibility and accountability. He urged all Zonal Railways to ensure effective and timely implementation of the Solid Waste Management Rules, 2026 across railway establishments and stressed the need for robust monitoring mechanisms and adoption of best practices to achieve improved waste management outcomes throughout the railway system.
As part of the programme, an environmental pledge was administered to officers and staff, encouraging active participation in efforts aimed at creating a cleaner, greener and more sustainable railway network.
The occasion also witnessed the presentation of Swachhata Pakhwada Awards, recognising outstanding efforts by Zonal Railways in promoting cleanliness and sanitation. North-Eastern Railway secured the first position, while North-Western Railway and Southern Railway were awarded the second and third positions respectively for their commendable performance during the cleanliness campaign.
The programme concluded with a renewed commitment towards strengthening environmental stewardship, promoting sustainable practices and advancing Indian Railways’ vision of building a cleaner, greener and environmentally responsible transport system for the nation.

articles.title: Surat Gets a Major Healthcare Boost: Prime Minister Narendra Modi Virtually Inaugurates ESIC's 220 Bedded Hospital
articles.full_content:
Multi-Specialty Hospital Will Serve Over 3.20 Lakh Insured Persons and 12 Lakh Beneficiaries Under the ESI Scheme Dignity of Labour at the Forefront: Construction Workers Felicitated and ESI Cash Benefits Disbursed at the Inauguration Venue
Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi virtually inaugurated the 220-bedded Employees’ State Insurance Corporation (ESIC) Hospital at Surat along with other development projects worth ₹ 18,800 Crore from Surat Indoor stadium today, marking yet another milestone in the Government’s sustained efforts to ensure quality healthcare for the organised workforce of the country.
सूरत को मिली ESIC अस्पताल की सौगात। श्रमिकों का सम्मान, मोदी सरकार की पहचान। pic.twitter.com/ZyJY1CLZQi
A separate function held at the ESIC Hospital venue was attended by the Director General ESIC, key employers, senior officers, and the general public, who witnessed a live telecast of the Prime Minister's virtual inaugural address. The occasion was further marked by felicitation of the construction workers who built the hospital, and direct disbursement of cash benefits to ESI beneficiaries, reaffirming the Government's commitment to the dignity and welfare of every worker.
The newly inaugurated 220-bedded ESIC Hospital at Surat, built over an 8.60-acre campus at a project cost of ₹216 Crore, will serve as a lifeline for approximately 3.20 lakh Insured Persons and around 12.16 lakh beneficiaries of the region. Strategically located just 20 minutes from Surat Railway Station, the hospital offers a comprehensive range of specialties including General Medicine, Surgery, Obstetrics & Gynaecology, Oncology, Psychiatry, Ophthalmology, ENT, and Dental care, supported by advanced Radiology, Pathology, and round-the-clock Emergency services.
The inauguration further strengthens Gujarat's expansive ESI healthcare ecosystem, which commenced in the state in October 1964 and today extends its social security umbrella to approximately 20.04 lakh Insured Persons across 34 districts, with the Surat Sub-Regional Office network serving workers across Surat, Navsari, and Valsad.
The inauguration of this hospital is a testament to the Government of India’s unwavering commitment to the welfare of the working class to further strengthen the healthcare ecosystem in the Surat region.

India Showcases Carbon Credit Trading Scheme and Renewable Energy Standards at WTO Trade and Environment Week 2026
articles.full_content:
India Highlights Sustainable Development and Clean Energy Transition Efforts at WTO Event India Showcases National Carbon Market Framework under Carbon Credit Trading Scheme
On the occasion of World Environment Day, India organized a special event titled "Showcase of India's Carbon Credit Trading Scheme and Standardization in Renewable Energy" during the WTO Trade and Environment Week 2026 in Geneva.
The event highlighted India's efforts towards sustainable development, clean energy transition and achievement of its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement. The session showcased key policy and regulatory initiatives aimed at supporting India's transition to a low-carbon economy.
The event featured presentations by experts and senior officials from the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC), Bureau of Energy Efficiency (BEE), Ministry of Power and Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE).
The programme commenced with a presentation on India's climate action and environmental sustainability initiatives. The presentation highlighted India's achievements under its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs), carbon market mechanisms and major environmental and climate change initiatives. It also underscored the principles of equity, Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC), and multilateral cooperation that guide India's approach towards climate action and sustainable development.
It was highlighted that India has been consistently achieving its climate commitments ahead of schedule. As of March 2026, the share of non-fossil fuel-based installed electricity generation capacity had reached 53.21 per cent against the target of 50 per cent by 2030, thereby achieving the target nearly five years in advance. Similarly, India's emissions intensity of GDP declined by 37.38 per cent between 2005 and 2022, surpassing the NDC target of a 33–35 per cent reduction by 2030 well ahead of the stipulated timeline.
The session also highlighted India's evolving carbon market architecture and efforts to establish standards and certification frameworks in emerging renewable energy sectors. India's national framework for the Indian Carbon Market, developed under the Carbon Credit Trading Scheme (CCTS), aims to establish a national electronic carbon credit trading platform and utilise market-based instruments to incentivise activities that contribute to greenhouse gas emission reduction.
The progress achieved under the National Green Hydrogen Mission was also presented, including the emission thresholds and technical criteria notified for classifying hydrogen as "Green Hydrogen". These standards are intended to provide transparency, credibility and certainty for producers, investors and consumers, while supporting the development of a robust green hydrogen ecosystem.

Panchayat Advancement Index Wins Gold Recognition at National e-Governance Awards 2026
articles.full_content:
The Panchayat Advancement Index (PAI), a flagship data-driven governance initiative of the Ministry of Panchayati Raj, has been selected for the Gold Award at the National Awards for e-Governance 2026 under Category VII – Digital Transformation through the Use of Data Analytics in Digital Platforms by Central Ministries, States and Union Territories. The award will be presented during the 29th National Conference on e-Governance, which will be held on 1–2 July 2026 in Jaipur, Rajasthan. The Conference is being jointly organised by the Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances (DARPG) and the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), in association with the Government of Rajasthan. The theme of this year’s Conference, “Viksit Bharat 2047: AI-Enabled, Data-Driven and Secure Digital Governance,” reflects the Government’s commitment to leveraging emerging technologies, data-driven decision-making and robust cybersecurity frameworks to deliver efficient, secure and citizen-centric public services.
The National Awards for e-Governance are conferred annually to recognise and promote excellence in the implementation of e-Governance initiatives across the country. In 2026, a total of 17 projects and initiatives have been selected for recognition across seven award categories. The Gold Award comprises a trophy, a citation, and a cash incentive of ₹10 lakh for further strengthening the initiative and advancing public welfare outcomes.
About Panchayat Advancement Index
The Panchayat Advancement Index (PAI) is India’s first comprehensive, data-driven framework for assessing and ranking the performance of Gram Panchayats across the nine themes of the Localisation of Sustainable Development Goals (LSDGs). PAI 2.0, the latest and significantly strengthened version of the Index, assesses more than 2.6 lakh Gram Panchayats against 150 indicators and 230 data points. Based on their composite scores, Gram Panchayats are classified into five performance categories. For Detailed Information on PAI click here

articles.title: TDB-DST supports M/s Greenjoules Private Limited, Pune for Commercialization of Indigenous Second-Generation Diesel Equivalent Biofuel Technology
articles.full_content:
As India accelerates its transition towards cleaner energy sources and circular economy-driven industrial growth, the Technology Development Board (TDB), Department of Science & Technology (DST), Government of India, has extended support to M/s Greenjoules Private Limited, Pune for its project titled “Manufacturing of 2nd Generation Diesel Equivalent Biofuel.” The project aims to establish a commercial-scale facility for the production of advanced biofuels derived from agricultural waste and agro-processing residues, contributing to India's energy security, waste-to-wealth mission, and net-zero aspirations.
The proposed project involves setting up a greenfield manufacturing facility for the production of Abhilasha Biofuels (ABF)—a next-generation renewable diesel and naphtha alternative developed entirely through indigenous technology. Designed as a "drop-in" fuel, ABF can directly replace conventional fossil-based diesel without requiring any modifications to existing engines, fuel systems, or distribution infrastructure, making it a practical and scalable solution for decarbonizing transportation and industrial energy consumption.
Developed entirely in India, the technology integrates innovative thermo-chemical conversion processes that transform agricultural residues and agro-industrial waste into high-quality renewable fuels. The process combines thermal cracking, catalytic upgrading, and downstream refining technologies to produce biofuels that meet established quality specifications comparable to their fossil-derived counterparts. The fuel has already been successfully produced from nearly forty different feedstocks, demonstrating remarkable flexibility and adaptability to India's diverse biomass resources.
A distinctive feature of the technology lies in Greenjoules' proprietary expertise in identifying optimal biomass blends and matching them with customized catalyst systems to maximize fuel yields and production efficiency. While individual process steps are known industrial practices, the company's unique integration of feedstock selection, catalyst optimization, and process engineering constitutes its core intellectual property and enables commercially viable production of advanced biofuels from low-value agricultural waste.
The project will further enhance the company's capabilities in producing renewable diesel, renewable naphtha, biochar, and gaseous fuels, creating a comprehensive waste-to-energy ecosystem. By converting agricultural residues that are often burned or discarded into valuable energy products, the technology addresses multiple national priorities simultaneously, including reduction of stubble burning, promotion of clean energy, strengthening of rural value chains, and lowering dependence on imported fossil fuels.
The initiative aligns closely with the Government of India's National Biofuel Policy, circular economy objectives, and broader efforts to promote sustainable industrial development through indigenous technolog

14th Meeting of India-Philippines Joint Working Group on Trade and Investment held in Manila
articles.full_content:
India, Philippines Explore New Avenues of Cooperation in Infrastructure, Digital Technologies and Pharmaceuticals India and Philippines Discuss Strengthening Trade Framework Through ASEAN-India Trade in Goods Agreement Review and Preferential Trade Agreement
The fourteenth meeting of the India-Philippines Joint Working Group on Trade and Investment (JWGTI) was convened on 05 June 2026, in Manila, Philippines. The Meeting was co-chaired by Mr. Amit Verma, Joint Secretary, Department of Commerce, Ministry of Commerce and Industry of the Republic of India, and Mr. Allan B. Gepty, Undersecretary, Department of Trade and Industry, International Trade Group. Representatives of concerned Ministries, Departments, and Organisations also participated through video conferencing.
Both sides acknowledged the robust growth of bilateral trade between India and the Philippines, which reached USD 3.9 billion in 2025–26, demonstrating a strong upward trajectory. Discussions covered trends in bilateral trade and investment, identification of priority products and services, and promotional activities and cooperation across various fields. The meeting also explored potential areas of collaboration in diverse sectors such as film, energy, construction and infrastructure, ICT/IT-BPM/AI, and pharmaceuticals. Both sides agreed that deeper cooperation in these areas could deliver sustained long-term benefits, enabling each country to pursue its developmental goals more effectively while reinforcing a framework of mutual support.
Improving the business environment to facilitate trade was another key focus. Discussions covered customs cooperation and facilitation, agricultural cooperation and market access for specific products, and trade settlement in national currencies.
The meeting also discussed the early conclusion of the ASEAN–India Trade in Goods Agreement (AITIGA) Review, and the engagement for a bilateral India–Philippines Preferential Trade Agreement (PTA) thereafter.
Overall, the meeting underscored the strategic importance of strengthening bilateral economic ties, reaffirming both countries’ commitment to building a more dynamic and mutually beneficial partnership.
On the sidelines of the 14th JWGTI, an interaction with Indian businesses operating in the Philippines was held on 04 June 2026, providing a platform for discussions on trade, investment, market opportunities, and ways to further deepen India–Philippines commercial ties.
The next meeting of the India-Philippines Joint Working Group on Trade and Investment (JWGTI) will be held in New Delhi, India. The JWGTI meeting served as a platform for India and the Philippines to exchange perspectives on global, regional, and bilateral developments, with a strong emphasis on enhancing trade in goods and services and investment ties.

articles.title: Department of Biotechnology organized Webinar on “Adapt Biomanufacturing to Address Climate Change”
articles.full_content:
On the eve of World Environment Day 2026, the Department of Biotechnology (DBT), Government of India, organized a webinar on “Adapt Biomanufacturing 2 Address Climate Change” aligned with the global theme of “Climate Action #NowForClimate ”.The session focused on DBT-driven initiatives that are contributing to biomanufacturing, biodiversity conservation, climate-resilient solutions, and sustainable development.
Dr. Sangita M. Kasture , Scientist G and Head, Biomanufacturing Directorate, DBT, welcomed the participants and provided an overview of the Department’s continued efforts to strengthen the biomanufacturing ecosystem through innovation-driven and sustainable biotechnology solutions. She highlighted the vision of the BioE3 Policy to advance high-performance biomanufacturing for sustainable fuels, chemicals, biomaterials, and other bio-based products that support environmental sustainability and climate-resilient agriculture.
Dr. Rajesh S. Gokhale , Secretary, DBT, DG, BRIC and Chairman, BIRAC highlighted the growing challenges posed by unsustainable material consumption, increasing waste generation and climate change.He underlined DBT’s efforts to support India’s climate and sustainability goals through high-performance biomanufacturing under the BioE3 Policy and emphasized the importance of scaling innovations through Biofoundries and Biomanufacturing Hubs to bridge research and industrial deployment. He called for translational research and industrial partnerships to accelerate the adoption of bio-based technologies.
Dr. Anil Prakash Joshi , an environmentalist with HESCO, Uttarakhand, delivered a keynote address on Himalayan Ecosystem Conservation. He emphasized community-led environmental stewardship, research relevance, sustainable resource management and climate-resilient development. Further recognizing the role of biotechnology in biomanufacturing, he stressed upon the footprint assessment of solutions. Dr Joshi said that integrated approaches are needed for biodiversity conservation, ecosystem restoration, and climate adaptation to safeguard ecological services and support resilient communities.
Webinar showcased the biotechnological developments happening through the Department’s activities for addressing climate change and environmental sustainability. This includes:Closing the Carbon Loop and Turning Carbon into Livelihoods, sustainable biomanufacturing approaches using microbial platforms; restoration of mangrove biodiversity in the Sundarbans through indigenous plant growth-promoting rhizobacteria; development of sustainable aviation fuel and green transportation fuels from biorenewable resources; applications of microbiome science for sustainability and bioeconomy development; ecosystem monitoring and the impacts of invasive species on ecologically sensitive wetlands; and the seedling traits and drought response in forests.

Mumbai Port Authority undertakes tree plantation drive under ‘Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam’ initiative on World Environment Day
articles.full_content:
Mumbai, 5 June 2026
Mumbai Port Authority organized a tree plantation drive on the occasion of World Environment Day 2026 under the nationwide ‘Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam’ initiative, reaffirming its commitment to environmental conservation and sustainable development.
The plantation programme, organized by the Civil Engineering Department of Mumbai Port Authority, was conducted at two locations in Mumbai and resulted in the plantation of a total of 1,500 saplings and plants. This includes 500 trees were planted at Ram Mandir, Cotton Green East, while 1,000 bamboo plants were planted along the footpaths at Sewri Gadi Adda, below Atal Setu, Sewri East.
The plantation activity at Cotton Green was carried out in association with FICCI FLO, while the Sewri plantation drive was organized in collaboration with the Director General of Shipping and the Amazing Nautical Association.
The ‘Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam’ initiative encourages citizens to plant a tree in honour of their mothers, symbolizing gratitude towards both motherhood and nature. Beyond increasing green cover, the initiative promotes environmental awareness and public participation in building a sustainable future.
Speaking on the occasion, Dr. P. N. Bahekar, Secretary, Mumbai Port Authority, said that planting a tree in the name of one’s mother is both a tribute and a commitment to protect nature and leave behind a healthier environment for future generations. He emphasised that development and environmental responsibility must go hand in hand and that every tree planted contributes towards cleaner air, greener public spaces and a more sustainable future.
The bamboo plantation at Sewri East is expected to enhance greenery along a key public movement corridor near Atal Setu, while the Cotton Green plantation will strengthen green cover in a port-linked urban area.
Mumbai Port Authority continues to undertake initiatives promoting sustainable port operations and environmental stewardship, reflecting its commitment to responsible and inclusive development.

Konkan Railway organizes trade meet at Ratnagiri to boost regional logistics and container traffic
articles.full_content:
Konkan Railway engages freight stakeholders at Ratnagiri Trade Meet; Aims to commence container services to JNPA by July 2026
Mumbai, 5 June 2026
Konkan Railway Corporation Limited (KRCL) organized a high-level trade meet at Ratnagiri on June 3, 2026, as part of its ongoing efforts to strengthen regional logistics infrastructure and enhance engagement with freight customers. The meeting provided a platform for interaction with key stakeholders and highlighted recent as well as upcoming freight infrastructure developments across the Konkan Railway network.
The meeting was chaired by Shri Santosh Kumar Jha, Chairman and Managing Director, KRCL. Senior officials including the Director (Way & Works), Chief Operations Manager, Regional Railway Managers of Ratnagiri and Karwar, and the Deputy General Manager of the Central Warehousing Corporation (CWC) attended the event.
A major focus of the deliberations was the significant potential for container traffic from the Ratnagiri region to Jawaharlal Nehru Port Authority (JNPA), Mumbai. Konkan Railway urged all stakeholders to work collectively towards commencing the first dedicated rail container service on this route by the first week of July 2026.
To support the initiative and ensure commercial viability, Central Warehousing Corporation (CWC) assured participants that competitive tariff rates would be offered to trade partners. The meeting witnessed active participation from freight customers engaged in the movement of fertilizers, foodgrains, and other commodities. Representatives from Jaigarh Port and Angre Port also attended, reflecting growing maritime-rail coordination for cargo movement.
Konkan Railway highlighted its ongoing capacity enhancement works and upcoming freight terminals aimed at providing faster, reliable, and cost effective logistics solutions. The discussions underscored the importance of coordinated efforts among railway authorities, ports, warehousing agencies, and trade stakeholders in developing an integrated logistics ecosystem.
Konkan Railway thanked participants for their support and feedback, reaffirming its commitment to efficient freight movement and stronger regional supply chains.

The Border Security Force, Western Command, observed ‘World Environment Day’ on 05 June 2026, with a large scale plantation drive, underscoring the force’s unwavering commitment to environment stewardship and sustainable living. Saplings of native plants including Neem, Peepal, Ashoka and fruits trees were planted at BSF Campus, Lakhnaur. Speaking on the occasion, the ADG Western Command emphasized the responsibility of every citizen and uniformed personnel to protect the environment, and reaffirmed BSF’s pledge towards a greener and sustainable India. The saplings are being planted every year by BSF in such campaigns during World Environment Day and monsoon season to ensure greater survivability.
This pan BSF mega plantation campaign was organized at all Frontiers up to BOP level from Kashmir to Gujarat, in which kith and kin of the troops were also participated with zest and fervor. Deserts, creeks and denuded patches are the areas of focus for this mega plantation drive in order to help combat environmental issues like desertification, soil erosion, etc. During this plantation drive more than 65000 saplings have been planted in entire western border.
ADG (Western Command) also emphasized that BSF has always been committed towards its social responsibilities along with its primary objective of defending the borders of the nation.

Central Region, Kochi, Kerala Postal Circle celebrates World Environment Day 2026. The focal point of this year's celebrations are Kerala's first ever bird sanctuary at Thattekkad and one of it's feathery inhabitant, the rare Srilankan Frogmouth.
Staff of Central Region, participated in a Swacchta activity inside the sanctuary. Staff also experienced a healthy mix of nature and wellness when they practiced Harit Yoga inside the Sanctuary.
A Special cover with a unique pictorial cancellation featuring the Sri Lankan Frogmouth, a rare and remarkable bird species inhabiting the forests of the Western Ghats and Sri Lanka was released in a Dak Chaupal organized at Thattecaud Bird Sanctuary on World Environment Day 2026.
The initiative aims to create awareness about the importance of biodiversity conservation and to recognize the dedicated efforts of Thattekkad Bird Sanctuary in protecting this unique species and its fragile habitat. The Sri Lankan Frogmouth is an iconic indicator of the rich ecological heritage of the Western Ghats, one of the world's biodiversity hotspots.
The Special Cover was formally released at Thattekkad Bird Sanctuary on 5 June 2026 at 10:00 AM in the presence of distinguished dignitaries including:
• Smt. T. Nirmala Devi, Postmaster General, Central Region, Kochi
• Sri Dean Kuriakose, Hon’ble Member of Parliament, Idukki Constituency
• Sri.Shibu Thekkumpuram, Hon’ble Member of Legislative Assembly, Kothamangalam Constituency
• Smt. Mary Kuriakose, President, Kuttampuzha Grama Panchayat
• Sri C T Ouseph, Asst. Wildlife Warden, Idukki Wildlife Division.
The event featured an awareness session on philately and other people friendly services offered by the Department of Posts, for 40 students of St. Stephen’s Girls High School, Keerampara, aimed at promoting interest in stamp collection and its educational value. In addition, officials of the Kerala Forest Department conducted an awareness programme on environmental conservation, reinforcing the importance of safeguarding biodiversity and natural habitats.
In connection with Special Cover release, Special campaigns for popularising diferent Post Office schemes were done in Kuttampuzha Grama Panchayath. All households in Ward 1 of Kuttampuzha panchayath was declared Sampoorna Bima Gram and after ensuring PLI/RPLI policies all eligible households. 60 new policies were canvassed as part of the campaign in addition to existing policies. Ward 13 of Kuttampuzha Panchayath was also declared as Sampoorna SSA ward and Lives of 83 girl children were secured under this campaign.
An Aadhaar camp was also conducted at sanctuary premises on 05.06.2026.
Through such initiatives, the Central Region, Kochi, Kerala Postal Circle, continues to promote both environmental awareness through philately and community well-being, reinforcing that the preservation of nature and the pursuit of wellness go hand in hand. The occasion served as a reminder of the collective responsibility to protect and conserve

The focal point of this year's celebrations are Kerala's first ever bird sanctuary at Thattekkad and one of it's feathery inhabitant, the rare Srilankan Frogmouth. Staff of Central Region, participated in a Swacchta activity inside the sanctuary.

articles.title: BRIC-IBSD Observes World Environment Day 2026
articles.full_content:
“Inspired by Nature. For Climate. For Our Future.”
BRIC – Institute of Bioresources and Sustainable Development (IBSD), Imphal, observed World Environment Day on June 5, 2026. In keeping with the theme designated by the United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP) for this year – “Inspired by Nature. For Climate. For Our Future.” – the occasion was marked with a tree plantation programme commencing at 9:00 AM at the institute campus situated at Takyelpat, Imphal.
This year’s theme, “Inspired by Nature. For Climate. For Our Future,” highlights the role of nature in addressing climate change. It is a reminder that many of the answers to the environmental challenges we face today already exist in natural systems, and that protecting and restoring nature is central to securing a livable future.
The programme was attended by faculty members, officers, staff, students, and researchers of the institute. Tree saplings, most of which are endangered and have medicinal value, were made available by the Department of Forest, Government of Manipur, whose cooperation is gratefully acknowledged.
As an institute engaged in research on the bioresources of the northeastern region, BRICIBSD considers the observance of such occasions an opportunity to reinforce its connection with environmental values and to encourage a culture of conservation among its members.

For Our Future.” – the occasion was marked with a tree plantation programme commencing at 9:00 AM at the institute campus situated at Takyelpat, Imphal.

Indian Army Leads Mass Plantation Drive Near Chumi Gyatse Holy Waterfalls on World Environment Day
articles.full_content:
Marking World Environment Day, the Indian Army's Gorkha Rifles, under the aegis of the Gajraj Corps, in collaboration with the Civil Administration, Thingbu, organized a large-scale plantation drive near the sacred Chumi Gyatse Holy Waterfalls in Arunachal Pradesh.
As part of the initiative, around 1,000 saplings of Rhododendron, Blue Pine and Deodar were planted in the ecologically sensitive Himalayan region situated at an altitude of approximately 12,000 feet. The plantation drive aimed to promote environmental conservation, enhance green cover and reinforce the commitment towards ecological sustainability in the high-altitude frontier areas.
The event witnessed enthusiastic participation from local residents, tourists, civil administration officials and Indian Army personnel, reflecting a collective resolve to preserve the fragile Himalayan ecosystem and safeguard its rich biodiversity for future generations.
The initiative highlighted the importance of community participation in environmental stewardship and underscored the Indian Army's continued commitment towards sustainable development and conservation efforts in the region.
The plantation drive was organized in line with the World Environment Day theme, "Inspired by Nature. For Climate. For Our Future," symbolizing a shared responsibility towards building a greener and more sustainable future.
Such collaborative efforts between the Indian Army, civil administration and local communities serve as a significant step towards environmental protection while strengthening the bond between the armed forces and the people of the region.

articles.full_content:
Over 400 Saplings Planted; Call Given for Collective Action Towards Environmental Sustainability
Marking World Environment Day-2026, the Steel Authority of India Limited-Bhilai Steel Plant organized a large-scale tree plantation drive in the Hospital Sector, Street-12 area of the Bhilai Steel Plant Township on Friday. The initiative, undertaken by the Horticulture Section of the Town Services Department, reinforced the plant’s commitment towards environmental conservation and sustainable development.
Director In-Charge of Bhilai Steel Plant, Chitta Ranjan Mohapatra, attended the programme as the Chief Guest. Executive Director (Human Resources) Pawan Kumar and Executive Director (Rowghat) Arun Kumar were present as Special Guests.
Addressing the gathering, Director In-Charge Chitta Ranjan Mohapatra extended greetings on the occasion of World Environment Day and underlined the growing environmental challenges confronting the world. He said that rising global temperatures and increasing air pollution have emerged as serious threats to human life and future generations. Stressing that environmental conservation is the need of the hour, he emphasized the importance of tree plantation, water conservation and sustainable use of natural resources in ensuring ecological balance.
Referring to the Prime Minister’s “Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam” campaign, he appealed to all employees and citizens to plant at least one sapling in the name of their mother. He stated that if every member of the Bhilai Steel Plant family plants and nurtures ten saplings annually, the collective effort would grow into lakhs of trees in the coming years and significantly strengthen environmental protection efforts.
Following the programme, dignitaries, students, environmental enthusiasts and citizens participated in the plantation campaign during which more than 400 saplings were planted across the area.
Among those present on the occasion were General Manager (Education) Shikha Dubey, General Manager In-Charge (Land Administration and Public Relations) Amulya Priyadarshi, General Managers of Town Services Department Vishnu K. Pathak, Atul Nautiyal, Vikash Chandra and Mahendra Sahu, Deputy General Manager Ravi Phule, Assistant General Manager Ramesh Kumar Gupta and several senior officials, employees and citizens. Students from Club Bulbul, Bharat Scouts and Guides, National Cadet Corps, sports academies and members of the Environment Department also participated enthusiastically in the campaign.
The plantation drive received active support from the Education Department, teachers, 50 children from National Cadet Corps and Bharat Scouts and Guides units, and 25 children from the Volleyball Academy.
The programme was organized under the leadership of General Manager In-Charge (Town Services Department) A. B. Srinivas and General Manager (Horticulture) Dr. N. K. Jain. The proceedings were conducted by Rajni Rajak of the Corporate Social Responsibility Department, who also proposed the vote of

BSF Chhattisgarh’s Green Pledge on World Environment Day – Over 5,000 Saplings Planted
articles.full_content:
On the occasion of World Environment Day, the Tactical Headquarters (Special Operations) BSF Chhattisgarh reaffirmed its commitment to environmental protection and green development by organizing a mega plantation drive. Under this initiative, more than 5,000 shady, medicinal, fruit-bearing, and ornamental saplings were planted across the Tactical Headquarters (Special Operations) BSF Chhattisgarh in Bhilai, as well as its subordinate battalions and units.
The plantation drive was inaugurated by Shri Girdhari Lal Meena, Deputy Inspector General (DIG), Tactical Headquarters (Special Operations) BSF Chhattisgarh, by planting a sapling. On this occasion, BSF officers, subordinate officers, and jawans participated with great enthusiasm, fulfilling their collective responsibility toward environmental conservation.
Speaking on the occasion, the Deputy Inspector General emphasized that plantation is not merely an event, but a crucial step toward ensuring a clean, safe, and balanced environment for future generations. He urged all personnel to ensure the regular care and protection of the planted saplings. He added "Plantation is not just a program, but a vital step toward securing a clean, safe, and balanced environment for our future generations."
BSF Chhattisgarh conducts large-scale plantation drives every year during the monsoon season, placing special emphasis on the survival and nurturing of the planted saplings. This year too, the drive will continue progressively from the BSF Tactical Headquarters down to the battalion and company levels.
At the conclusion of the program, the BSF family extended its warm wishes to all citizens on World Environment Day, delivering a strong message of maximizing plantation and public participation for environmental conservation. This plantation drive is not only a significant step toward ecological preservation but also a powerful example of BSF's commitment to social responsibility, public awareness, and sustainable development.

articles.title: NHAI Organises “Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam” Plantation Drive on World Environment Day 2026
articles.full_content:
05 June 2026, Vijayawada.
As part of World Environment Day 2026, NHAI (National Highways Authority of India) organized the “Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam” plantation drive at various Toll Plazas across the State of Andhra Pradesh. Among them, a major plantation programme was conducted at Kaza Toll Plaza under the guidance of the Regional Officer, Shri R.K. Singh, IES, CGM(T).
The programme witnessed the presence of chief guests Shri S. Balaji, Asst. Inspector General & Forests, MOEF&CC, IRO Chennai; Shri C. Murali Krishna, Addl. Director & Scientist-E, MOEF&CC.
The programme commenced with the message sent by NHAI Chairman Shri Santosh Kumar Yadav from New Delhi on the occasion of World Environment Day, which was conveyed by Dr. K. Suryanarayana, Retd. IES, Joint Advisor (Environment & Plantation).
Addressing the gathering, Shri R.K. Singh, IES, CGM(T) NHAI RO Vijayawada, stated that NHAI is committed to creating green and healthy highways for the people of the State. Andhra Pradesh has a total National Highway network of 8,360 kms, out of which 4,022 kms are under the jurisdiction of NHAI. He stated that during the period from 2010 to 2025, around 18 lakh seedlings have been planted along National Highways through avenue and median plantations, and the same are being maintained to mitigate the adverse impacts of climate change and ensure safer travel for highway users.
Further, during the current year 2026–27, a target of 3.86 lakh saplings has been set for avenue and median plantation. Today, under the theme “Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam” initiative, about 3,200 saplings have been planted across the State of Andhra Pradesh.
Shri R.K. Singh, IES, CGM(T) NHAI RO Vijayawada, further stated that the goal of “Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam” is to encourage every citizen to plant a sapling in tribute to his or her mother. He said the programme reflects India’s culture and heritage by honouring Mother Earth through plantation activities, promoting awareness on nature conservation and encouraging the concept of reduce, reuse and recycle. He also called upon students to actively participate and help create a green army for the future.
The dignitaries highlighted the importance of environmental protection, biodiversity conservation, pollution control and sustainable living practices for safeguarding future generations. They stressed the need to increase green cover, conserve natural resources and create greater public awareness on environmental responsibility. They also encouraged everyone to actively participate in plantation drives and contribute towards building a cleaner, greener, healthier and more sustainable future.
Shri M.K. Chowdary, General Manager; Shri T. Srinivas, DGM and Shri M. Vidyasagar, Project Director; were among the other dignitaries present on the occasion.

Among them, a major plantation programme was conducted at Kaza Toll Plaza under the guidance of the Regional Officer, Shri R.K. The programme witnessed the presence of chief guests Shri S.

articles.title: NHAI Regional Office Dehradun Plants 570 Saplings Under “Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam” Campaign on World Environment Day
articles.full_content:
* A sapling in the name of mothers, a green gift to the environment by RO Dehradun * A meaningful initiative by NHAI Regional Office Dehradun on World Environment Day * 570 saplings planted under the “Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam” campaign, promoting sustainable development and green highways
In an era of rapid development and expanding modern infrastructure, environmental conservation has become more important than ever. Taking this vision forward, the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI), Regional Office Dehradun, organized a large-scale plantation drive under the “Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam” campaign on the occasion of World Environment Day.
As part of the initiative, a total of 570 saplings were planted at various locations by the project implementation units (PIUs) under Regional Office Dehradun, including PIU Vasant Vihar, Najibabad, Rudrapur and Roorkee. Plantation activities were carried out at different locations including Gram Palwal, Jhajra Chowk, Dehradun, where officers, employees and staff members from 13 toll plazas under RO Dehradun actively participated and spread the message of environmental conservation.
National highways today are not just about better connectivity and smoother travel experiences; they are also emerging as symbols of balanced development and environmental responsibility. With this vision, NHAI is continuously promoting the development of green, safe and environment-friendly highways across the country.
Under the “Meri LiFE” (Lifestyle for Environment) initiative, special emphasis is being laid on plantation drives, development of green belts, biodiversity conservation and adoption of eco-friendly construction practices. NHAI’s efforts are focused on ensuring that infrastructure development progresses alongside environmental sustainability and ecological balance.
The plantation drive organized on World Environment Day reflected a collective commitment towards environmental responsibility and securing a greener future for the coming generations. The 570 saplings planted during the campaign are expected to contribute towards cleaner air, greener surroundings and a healthier environment in the years to come.
On the occasion, Regional Officer, Uttarakhand, Shri Dinesh Kumar Chaturvedi stated that along with the development of national highways, environmental conservation also remains one of NHAI’s key priorities. He added that campaigns like “Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam” not only encourage environmental awareness but also create an emotional connection between society and nature.
The programme was attended by Smt. Sarita Budiyal, Deputy General Manager (Technical); Shri Sourabh Singh, Project Director, PIU Vasant Vihar; Shri Navneet Nautiyal, Manager (Technical); Smt. Shalu Chauhan, Manager (Technical); Shri Divyanshu, Manager (Technical); Shri Vivek Singh, Site Engineer; and Ms. Keerthi Vysyaraju, Plantation Associate, along with other officials and staff members.

articles.title: CSIR-IIP Celebrates 67th Foundation Day and World Environment Day
articles.full_content:
* Director, DEAL-DRDO, Shri Manoj Kumar Dhaka graces the occasion as Chief Guest * Institute highlights achievements in research, innovation, sustainability and carbon footprint reduction
CSIR-Indian Institute of Petroleum (CSIR-IIP), a premier research and development institution under the Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR), celebrated its 67th Foundation Day and World Environment Day with enthusiasm and commitment towards scientific excellence and environmental sustainability.
Shri Manoj Kumar Dhaka, Outstanding Scientist and Director, Defence Electronics Applications Laboratory (DEAL), Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO), Dehradun, graced the occasion as the Chief Guest. The celebration reflected the institute’s rich legacy of pioneering research, innovative technologies and strong industry collaborations since its establishment on 14 April 1960.
Welcoming the guests and participants, Dr. H. S. Bisht, Director, CSIR-IIP, highlighted the institute’s major developments and achievements during the past year. He elaborated on the institute’s contributions in research, technology development and its continued efforts towards addressing national priorities in the energy and petroleum sectors.
Addressing the gathering, Shri Manoj Kumar Dhaka extended his best wishes to the institute on its 67th Foundation Day. In his insightful keynote address, he highlighted the activities and significant work carried out by DEAL in the field of satellite-based communication systems and emphasized the importance of technological innovation in strengthening national capabilities.
Dr. Sanat Kumar, Chief Scientist, presented an overview of World Environment Day and highlighted various projects and initiatives undertaken by CSIR-IIP to reduce carbon footprint and promote sustainable development. He underlined the institute’s commitment to environmentally responsible research and technology solutions.
On the occasion, a Swachhta Abhiyan Pledge was administered to all participants, reaffirming their commitment towards cleanliness, environmental protection and sustainable living.
As part of the World Environment Day celebrations, a plantation drive was organized within the institute campus under the supervision of Dr. Sunil Kumar Suman, Senior Principal Scientist. Scientists, officers, staff members and students enthusiastically participated in the drive, contributing to the institute’s ongoing efforts towards environmental conservation.
The programme was coordinated by the Head, Science Communication and Dissemination Directorate, and concluded with a renewed commitment to scientific innovation, environmental stewardship and nation-building.
