Shri Manoj Kumar Dhaka, Outstanding Scientist and Director, Defence Electronics Applications Laboratory (DEAL), Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO), Dehradun, graced the occasion as the Chief Guest. The celebration reflected the institute’s rich legacy of pioneering research, innovative technologies and strong industry collaborations since its establishment on 14 April 1960.

articles.title: "National Workshop on Convergence of Artificial Intelligence, Material Science and Cyber Security for Smart Manufacturing" in Industry 4.0 Organized at Doon University
articles.full_content:
- Besides Doon University and IIT Madras, six other institutions are participating in the workshop.
On Friday, Doon University and IIT Madras jointly organized a two-day National Workshop on the Convergence of Artificial Intelligence, Material Science and Cyber Security for Smart Manufacturing in Industry 4.0 under the Partnership for Accelerated Innovation and Research (PAIR) programme of the Anusandhan National Research Foundation (ANRF), Government of India. The workshop aims to train participants on the applications and potential uses of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in smart manufacturing, materials, sensors, and Industry 4.0.
Apart from Doon University and IIT Madras, six other institutions are participating in the workshop, including the Indian Institute of Information Technology (IIIT) Tiruchirappalli, Central University of Tamil Nadu, National Institute of Technology (NIT) Puducherry, NIT Mizoram, Bharathiar University Coimbatore, and Cochin University of Science and Technology.
Vice-Chancellor of Doon University, Professor Surekha Dangwal, stated that under the prestigious ANRF scheme, Doon University was inducted into IIT Madras’ “Partnership for Accelerated Innovation and Research (PAIR)” network in February 2025. She thanked IIT Madras Director Professor V. Kamakoti for including the university in the network. Professor Dangwal explained that the partnership aims to promote interdisciplinary collaboration and innovation in research in line with the vision of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
Dr. Charu Dwivedi, Faculty Member in the Department of Chemistry at Doon University and Chief Coordinator of the PAIR Programme for the university, informed that the ANRF-PAIR Programme was launched by the Anusandhan National Research Foundation in November 2024. She said that experts from IIT Madras and industry would conduct various sessions during the workshop. Key resource persons include three professors from IIT Madras—Professor John Augustine, Professor G.L. Samuel, and Dr. Charchit Kumar; Dr. Bhupesh Janoti from the Data Security Council of India; Dr. Anand Phadnis from Siemens Industry; and Dr. Mayank Swarnkar from IIT-BHU.
The workshop features sessions on important topics such as Robust and Secure Multi-Agent Systems, Role of AI and Metrology in Smart Manufacturing, Advanced Sensors for Smart Manufacturing Systems, Security for Industry 5.0 AI-Native Communication, and Building Cyber-Physical Resilience in Industry 4.0.
Professor Dangwal also noted that the Anusandhan National Research Foundation (ANRF) was established under the ANRF Act, 2023, passed by the Parliament of India. In line with the Prime Minister’s vision, ANRF aims to promote, develop, and nurture a culture of research and innovation in universities, colleges, research institutions, and R&D laboratories across the country.

articles.full_content:
E85-powered vehicles run on domestically produced ethanol derived from the sweat and toil of Indian farmers: Sh. Hardeep Singh Puri E85 is priced nearly ₹20 per litre lower than conventional petrol to pass on the economic benefits of domestically produced ethanol to consumers E85 की असली ताकत यह है—न आयातित तेल , न विदेशी खनिज; भारत की उपज, भारत की प्रगति;
Union Minister for Petroleum and Natural Gas Shri Hardeep Singh Puri launched E85 fuel at an IndianOil retail outlet in New Delhi today on the occasion of World Environment Day 2026. Senior officials of the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas, and CMDs of public sector OMCs were also present during the event.
E85 is a high-ethanol blended fuel comprising 80–85 per cent ethanol and 14–19 per cent petrol, specifically designed for use in flex-fuel vehicles. The initiative aims to facilitate the adoption of Flex-Fuel Vehicles (FFVs), which are capable of operating on ethanol blends from E20 to E100, without restricting consumers to a single blend.
The rollout commences across 48 Public Sector OMC’s retail outlets (ROs) in the country, enabling flex-fuel vehicle users to access this cleaner fuel. The initiative is slated for nationwide expansion.The same would be scaled up to 500 ROs by December 2026 and about 5000 ROs by December 2027 and help raise India’s aggregate ethanol blending levels to nearly 26 per cent by 2030-31.
Speaking at the event, the Minister said India has demonstrated its ability to successfully balance the “Energy Trilemma” of energy availability, affordability and sustainability, while ensuring stable fuel supplies, protecting consumers from global energy volatility to the maximum possible extent. He noted that India has one of the lowest increases of fuel prices since February 2026.
Highlighting that ethanol blending has increased from 1.53 per cent in 2014 to 20 per cent today, with the target achieved five years ahead of schedule, he stated that this transformation has helped save over ₹1.84 lakh crore in foreign exchange and substituted nearly 302 lakh metric tonnes of crude oil imports. This has been driven by the contribution of India’s farmers, who have evolved from being the country’s “Annadatas” to becoming its “Urjadatas” as well.
Benefits of E85:
• E85 is priced nearly ₹20 per litre lower than conventional petrol to ensure that the economic benefits of domestically produced ethanol are passed on to consumers.
• Flex-fuel vehicles operating on E85 can reduce lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions by around 61 per cent compared to conventional petrol vehicles.
• With a Research Octane Number (RON) of about 108, ethanol offers superior knock resistance that allows engines to operate at higher compression ratios and optimized ignition timing.
• Higher ethanol blends promote cleaner and more complete combustion, resulting in near-zero particulate matter emissions contributing to improved urban air quality.

articles.title: CSIR-NEERI Celebrates World Environment Day, Focuses on Environmental Surveillance and Public Health
articles.full_content:
Nagpur, 5 June 2026
CSIR-National Environmental Engineering Research Institute (CSIR-NEERI) celebrated World Environment Day on 5 June 2026. Dr. Rakesh Mishra, Director, Tata Institute for Genetics and Society (TIGS), Bengaluru and former Director, CSIR-Centre for Cellular & Molecular Biology (CSIR-CCMB), Hyderabad, was the Chief Guest. Dr. S. Venkata Mohan, Director, CSIR-NEERI, and Shri Prakash Kumbhare, Scientist-F, CSIR-NEERI, were also present.
Delivering a lecture on ‘Environmental Surveillance and One Health’, Dr. Mishra highlighted the role of metagenomics and environmental surveillance in monitoring pathogens, antimicrobial resistance (AMR) and ecosystem health. Referring to wastewater surveillance during the COVID-19 pandemic, he said environmental monitoring enabled early detection of infection trends and viral variants, supporting timely public health interventions.
He emphasized that AMR is an emerging global challenge and highlighted national initiatives such as the Alliance for Pathogen Surveillance Innovations (APSI) and the National One Health Mission for monitoring pathogens through wastewater, air and other environmental matrices. Stressing the One Health approach, he said human, animal and environmental health are closely interconnected and require coordinated action.
Dr. Mishra also underscored the importance of collaboration among scientific institutions, government agencies and communities for building resilient surveillance systems and strengthening health preparedness.
In his welcome address, Dr. S. Venkata Mohan highlighted CSIR-NEERI’s contributions in air and water quality management, wastewater treatment, waste valorization and eco-restoration, reaffirming the institute’s commitment to sustainable environmental solutions.
On the occasion, an MoU was signed between CSIR-NEERI and TIGS to strengthen collaborative research and innovation. Two White Papers — ‘Industrial Ecology in the Indian Context’ and ‘Environmental Health Risk Atlas and Action Framework for the Vidarbha Region’ — were released. The book ‘Fungal Diversity in CSIR-NEERI Nagpur’ was also unveiled. Certificates of Excellence were presented to students participating in the Student Research & Innovation Expo for their innovative ideas and science models.

World Environment Day: NHAI Launches Green Drives and Eco-Friendly Cloth Bags Distribution across National Highways
articles.full_content:
To commemorate World Environment Day, the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) Regional Office, Madurai organized a series of environmental sustainability drives across various Highway projects in southern Tamil Nadu, reinforcing its commitment to green infrastructure. As part of its long-term conservation effort, NHAI has successfully planted over 2000 saplings, sowed 100 palm seeds, and distributed 600 eco-friendly cloth bags to commuters across various National Highway sections today.
The initiative highlights NHAI’s focus on sustainable infrastructure development to ecological preservation, aligning closely with Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). The eco-friendly bags distribution and plantation drives with toll plaza commuters, local villagers, and students at major intersections to sensitize them on eliminating single-use plastics and promoting sustainable habits. An official pledge to adopt eco-friendly and sustainable ways of living was also administered with students and staff during the event.
Underscoring the strategic integration of safety and sustainability, the Regional Officer, NHAI Madurai, emphasized the organization's focus on ecological balance. He said that "Our responsibility extends far beyond building world-class Highways; NHAI is dedicated to building sustainable corridors. Achieving substantial green cover across our networks is a top priority. We are actively maintaining major NH stretches with an approx 80% green cover and executing scientific tree transplants with high survival rates and building rain water harvesting structures. These activities under the Regional Office, Madurai are the testament to our proactive efforts towards environment and ensuring economic growth goes hand-in-hand with preservation. Furthermore, these initiatives will help to create awareness among students and the public, motivating them to adopt eco-friendly day to day practices."
Speaking on the occasion, the Joint Adviser, NHAI informed that,
"The 54th World Environment Day serves as a vital reminder to integrate eco-friendly ideas into our core Highway operations. By combining active plantation drives with community-focused awareness initiatives like distributing eco-friendly cloth bags to staff, the public, and students, NHAI is fostering a collective culture of environmental responsibility. A Greener Highway is inherently a safer, more sustainable highway for future generations. Today, we took a joint pledge with students and commuters to make conscious, eco-friendly changes in our daily lives."
The plantation drives include native species like Neem, Pungam, Naval, Palm,etc and plantation will continue across various ongoing project units in the region, backed by a proactive institutional monitoring mechanism and regular maintenance works to ensure high survival rates of the saplings.

articles.title: INDIAN ARMY COMMEMORATES WORLD ENVIRONMENT DAY 2026 WITH SWACHHATA CAMPAIGN AND MASS TREE PLANTATION DRIVE
articles.full_content:
Agartala, 5 June 2026: Red Shield Division under the Spear Corps, commemorated World Environment Day 2026 with a comprehensive Swachhata Campaign and Mass Tree Plantation Drive, reaffirming the Indian Army's commitment towards environmental conservation and sustainable development.
The initiative was conducted in consonance with the global observance of World Environment Day and aimed at promoting environmental stewardship, enhancing green cover and fostering awareness on climate action among troops, families and the local community.
The programme commenced with a large-scale tree plantation drive across military stations and selected community areas. A variety of native and climate-resilient saplings, including Neem, Peepal and Gulmohar, were planted to strengthen biodiversity and contribute towards long-term ecological sustainability. The plantation drive symbolised a collective commitment to creating a greener and healthier future.
Simultaneously, personnel of the Brigade undertook an extensive Swachhata Campaign focused on cleanliness, waste management and environmental responsibility. The drive included the collection and segregation of waste into recyclable, biodegradable and non-recyclable categories. Special emphasis was laid on the elimination of single-use plastics and the promotion of environmentally responsible practices. The collected recyclable waste was handed over to authorised recycling agencies for scientific disposal and processing.
To ensure the sustained growth and maintenance of the newly planted saplings, dedicated teams have been tasked with their care and upkeep. The event also featured an environmental awareness session highlighting the importance of climate action, water conservation, waste segregation and sustainable living practices.
The observance concluded with participants taking a pledge to adopt eco-friendly habits, reduce plastic consumption and contribute actively towards environmental conservation. Through such initiatives, the Indian Army continues to demonstrate its unwavering commitment to nation-building, environmental protection and the creation of a sustainable future for generations to come.

articles.title: ICAR NEH Sikkim Centre Celebrates World Environment Day 2026 with a Massive Plantation Drive
articles.full_content:
The ICAR Research Complex for North Eastern Hill Region, Sikkim Centre, celebrated World Environment Day - 2026 with a large-scale mass plantation drive at the ICAR NEH Sikkim Centre Research Farm, Tadong, in collaboration with several other line departments and research organisations, including the National Horticulture Board, Spices Board – Indian Cardamom Research Institute, Regional Ayurveda Research Institute, and G.B. Pant National Institute of Himalayan Environment of Govt. of India. The programme was launched under the ongoing Khet Bachao Abhiyan implemented by the Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare, Government of India.
The event witnessed active participation from Shri Sansar Ahmad, JD, NHB; Dr. John Varghese, Deputy Director, ICRI, Spices Board; Dr. A J V Sai Prasad, Asstt. Director In-charge, RARI, Gangtok; Dr. J C Arya, Research Officer Botany, RARI, Gangtok; Dr. Lizima C, Research Officer Ayurveda, RARI, Gangtok; along with scientists, technical staff, and officials from the ICAR RC for NEH Sikkim Centre.
As part of the mass plantation drive, a diverse range of economically important, horticultural, agroforestry, and environmentally beneficial species were planted. The plantation included 5,000 pineapple suckers, 6,000 large cardamom saplings, 300 papino (Solanum muricatum) seedlings, 60 Nebaro (Ficus roxburghii), 120 Gagun (Saurauia nepalensis), 60 Tejpatta (Cinnamomum tamala), and several bamboo species including Bambusa balcooa, Bambusa wamin, Bambusa tulda, Dendrocalamus hamiltonii, Bambusa nutans, Bambusa vulgaris, and Bambusa multiplex. In addition, 156 apple ber, 100 mahogany, 100 neem, 5 low-chill apple, and 500 moringa saplings were also planted.
The mass plantation initiative aimed to promote environmental conservation, enhance biodiversity, strengthen ecosystem resilience, and support climate change mitigation efforts. The collaborative effort reflects the commitment of scientific institutions and other stakeholder organizations to sustainable natural resource management and the vision of a greener and more climate-resilient Sikkim.
The programme concluded with a collective pledge by all participants to promote environmental stewardship and undertake sustained efforts towards ecological restoration and sustainable development in the Sikkim Himalayan region.

articles.title: Parliamentary Consultative Committee meeting under MoHFW held at Shillong
articles.full_content:
The Ministry of Health & Family Welfare, Parliamentary Consultative Committee meeting was held on 5th Jun 2026 at Courtyard by Marriott, Shillong. The committee headed by Chairman Hon'ble Union Cabinet Minister of Health and Family Welfare & Minister of Chemicals and Fertilizers Shri. Jagat Prakash Nadda, along with Hon'ble Minister of State, Ministry of Health and Family Welfare & Minister of State, Ministry of Chemicals and Fertilizers Smt. Anupriya Patel discussed various matters related to Child Health Program in India and ways to improve various aspects of Child Health policies in India.
The meeting was also attended by Hon'ble Members of Parliament from Lok Sabha including Dr. CN Manjunath, Dr. Sanjay Jaiswal, Shri. Rajabhau Parag Prakash Waje, Shri. Satish Kumar Gautam, Shri. Brijendra Singh Ola, Shri. Anoop Pradhan, Dr. Sharmila Sarkar and Rajya Sabha members including Dr. Kalpana Saini, Shri. Haris Beeran, Dr. Parmar Jashvantsinh Salamsinh, Dr. Radha Mohan Das Agarwal, Smt. Renuka Chowdhary and Smt. Rajathi. Union Secretary MoHFW, Smt. Punya Salila Srivastava along with Secretary of the Department of Health Research (DHR) within the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare (MoHFW) & the Director General of the Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR) Dr. Rajiv Bhal and Director NEIGRIHMS Prof. Nalin Mehta were also present during the deliberations.
The members also visited NEIGRIHMS, Shillong. During the visit at NEIGRIHMS the committee took overview of infrastructure & functioning of hospital along with special focus of child health care services offered at NEIGRIHMS. The members visited the pediatric OPD & ward and interacted with patients and were satisfied with the services and health care delivery offered for the pediatric cases at NEIGRIHMS. The Members along with Union Secretary, MoHFW, Smt. Punya Salila Srivastava also celebrated the World Environment Day, 5th Jun at NEIGRIHMS by Tree Plantation Drive and planted various saplings inside the campus. All the honorable Members of Parliament along with Secretary, MoHFW, Smt. Punya Salila Srivastava echoed the theme of the world environment day 2026 "Inspired by Nature. For Climate. For Our Future." The Tree plantation drive was a small step by the ministry that emphasizes the importance of nature for sustained & climate-resilient planet for the next century.

INDIRA GANDHI NATIONAL FOREST ACADEMY, DEHRADUN ORGANIZES SPECIAL LECTURE ON WORLD ENVIRONMENT DAY
articles.full_content:
The Indira Gandhi National Forest Academy (IGNFA), Dehradun, organized a Special Lecture on the occasion of World Environment Day at the Hari Singh Auditorium. Shri Bhaskar Khulbe, IAS (Retd.), former Secretary, PMO and Adviser to the Prime Minister of India delivered the special lecture as the chief guest. The programme commenced with the traditional lighting of the lamp, followed by a welcome address and introduction of the distinguished speaker. He also planted a sapling on this occasion.
Addressing the gathering, Shri Bhaskar Khulbe shared valuable insights on environmental governance, sustainable development, and the role of public institutions in addressing contemporary environmental challenges. Drawing from his vast experience in public administration and policymaking, he emphasized the importance of balancing conservation objectives with developmental aspirations. He highlighted the need for innovative approaches to environmental management, community participation, and responsible stewardship of natural resources.
Shri Khulbe also spoke about his involvement in initiatives related to plastic waste management in eco-sensitive zones and sustainable development planning in the Himalayan region, including the reconstruction efforts at Kedarnath and Badrinath. He underscored the significance of environmental leadership and encouraged the probationers to uphold the values of integrity, commitment, and public service throughout their careers.
Smt. Bharati, the Director of IGNFA, faculty members, and probationers expressed their gratitude to Shri Khulbe for sharing his experiences and perspectives. The lecture provided an enriching opportunity for the probationers to learn from a distinguished civil servant whose career exemplifies dedication to nation-building, governance reforms, and environmental sustainability.
The programme concluded with an interactive session, where probationers engaged with the speaker on issues related to environmental conservation, governance, climate resilience, and sustainable development.
The event formed part of IGNFA's continued efforts to expose future forest officers to diverse perspectives and inspire them to contribute effectively towards the conservation and sustainable management of India's natural resources.
The occasion was well attended by the Indian Forest Service (IFS) Probationers, faculty members of IGNFA and CASFoS Dehradun, officers, staff, and distinguished guests.

articles.title: On World Environment Day, Union Agriculture Minister Shri Shivraj Singh Chouhan plants tree at Smart City Park in Bhopal under the ‘Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam’ campaign
articles.full_content:
Trees mean the future. Let us turn environment protection into a mass movement : Shri Shivraj Singh Chouhan Make tree plantation a regular part of life because if there are trees, we exist : Shri Chouhan Join the ‘Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam’ campaign and contribute to environment protection : Union Agriculture Minister Shri Shivraj Singh Chouhan The resolve of planting a tree every day has continued without a break for the last five and a half years : Shri Shivraj Singh Chouhan Trees are not just greenery; they are the foundations of life, biodiversity and the security of our future : Shri Chouhan
On the occasion of World Environment Day, Union Minister for Agriculture and Farmers’ Welfare, and Rural Development, Shri Shivraj Singh Chouhan planted a tree at the Smart City Park in Bhopal under the ‘Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam’ campaign launched by Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi. While greeting the people of the state and the country on World Environment Day, Union Minister Shri Shivraj Chouhan said that life on Earth will remain safe if our environment remains safe. Tree plantation is not just an act of planting a sapling but a campaign to protect humanity, nature and future generations. He said that if trees are saved, water, forests, land and biodiversity will remain protected. There will be rainfall, rivers will be saved, soil will be conserved and there will be continuity of life. Shri Chouhan appealed to all citizens to consider environment protection as their personal responsibility and make tree plantation a regular part of life because if there are trees, we exist.
One sapling grows into a grand banyan tree and serves Nature
While looking at a banyan tree planted in 2021 at the Smart City Park, Union Minister Shri Shivraj Singh Chouhan said that five years ago, he had taken a resolve from Amarkantak to plant a tree every day, and the first sapling he planted after coming to Bhopal was this one. He said that today this sapling has grown into a massive banyan tree. Seeing birds chirping on that tree, the activities of living beings and the vibrant form of nature, he felt immense satisfaction and joy. Comparing trees with children, he said that just as nurturing children helps them grow, similarly a sapling grows into a huge tree over time and serves society and nature.
Take a resolve to plant trees on special occasions
Union Agriculture Minister Shri Shivraj Singh Chouhan appealed to citizens that if daily tree plantation is not possible, they should at least plant trees on special occasions in life. Birthdays, children’s birthdays, marriages, wedding anniversaries, in memory of parents and other family occasions should be linked with tree plantation. He said that through Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi’s ‘Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam’ campaign, lakhs of people across the country are planting trees in honour of their mothers. He added that he also planted a tree in the memory of his late mother Smt. Sundar Devi Ji.
Tree plantation campaign has gone from a resolve

Ramasubramanian emphasised the need for stronger protection of existing natural ecosystems, stricter regulation of construction around water bodies and actionable recommendations focused on sustainable urban development Member, Justice (Dr.) Bidyut Ranjan Sarangi highlighted the need to balance urban development with environmental protection, urging collective action to conserve nature Secretary General, Shri Bharat Lal expressed concern over the rising number of deaths due to heat waves across the country despite efforts to mitigate its impact Among various suggestions, improving heat wave mortality and morbidity surveillance through a unified, scientifically validated reporting and data management system highlighted
The National Human Rights Commission (NHRC), India organised a meeting of its Core Group on Environment & Climate on the theme ‘Heat Wave and its Mitigation in Urban Areas’ in hybrid mode at Manav Adhikar Bhavan, New Delhi. The meet generated lots of interest among various stakeholders and saw a very meaningful participation. Chairperson, Justice V. Ramasubramanian while presiding the meeting, said that there is a recurring annual discourse on pollution in winter and heat waves in summer for some time now without any visible effects of the mitigation efforts to protect human life from the impact of these crises. NHRC Members, Justice (Dr.) Bidyut Ranjan Sarangi, Smt. Vijaya Bharathi Sayani; Secretary General, Shri Bharat Lal; DG (I), Smt. Anupama Nilekar Chandra; Joint Secretary, Smt. Saidingpuii Chhakchhuak; Core Group members, Special Rapporteurs, Special Monitors, senior functionaries of Government of India and various parastatal organisations; Municipal Commissioners of Ahmedabad and Indore, eminent domain experts and members of civil society organisations were present in the meeting.
Justice Ramasubramanian said that the evolution of human rights initially focused on civil, political, social, cultural and economic rights after the First and Second World Wars, while environmental rights remained largely overlooked. Although the Universal Declaration of Human Rights (UDHR) was adopted in 1948, serious discourse on environmental rights began around 1970 following growing awareness of environmental degradation and major ecological disasters.
He said that the industrial revolution contributed so much to climate change and the consequent heat wave that we started realising it after it really impacted our lives. He said that if you read about the Gandhian economy, he never contemplated the industrialisation of the entire country. He thought that every village should be self-reliant, but the reverse happened, resulting in migration from villages to urban areas. He said that unliveable rural conditions and the growth of ‘concrete jungles’ in cities have further contributed to rising temperatures and heat wave-related challenges. “TDB-DST supports M/s Bioneemtec India Private Limited, Chennai for Commercialization of Green Chemistry-Based Pharmaceutical Manufacturing Technologies”
articles.full_content:
As India strengthens its position as a global pharmaceutical manufacturing powerhouse, the Technology Development Board (TDB), Department of Science & Technology (DST), Government of India, has extended support to M/s Bioneemtec India Private Limited, Chennai for the project titled “Manufacturing of Minoxidil, Tranexamic Acid, Dequalinium Chloride, their Intermediates and Drug Liposomes.” The project seeks to commercialize indigenous green chemistry technologies for the production of critical pharmaceutical ingredients and advanced drug delivery systems, contributing to import substitution, sustainable manufacturing, and healthcare security.
The project focuses on establishing advanced manufacturing capabilities for pharmaceutical intermediates, Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs), and novel drug delivery systems through a combination of batch and continuous-flow chemistry technologies. By adopting modern process technologies, the initiative aims to improve production efficiency, product quality, operational safety, and environmental sustainability while reducing dependence on imported pharmaceutical intermediates.
A key component of the project is the commercialization of an indigenous process for manufacturing Tranexamic Acid, an important therapeutic molecule used in the management of bleeding disorders. Developed using a cost-effective and environmentally responsible synthesis route, the technology is designed to deliver high-purity products while minimizing waste generation and process complexity. The project will also support the domestic production of Minoxidil and Dequalinium Chloride along with their key intermediates, strengthening local manufacturing capabilities in pharmaceutical value chains that presently depend substantially on imports.
Adding a strong innovation dimension, Bioneemtec has developed proprietary Drug Liposome APIs based on advanced nanotechnology platforms. These liposomal formulations are being developed for therapeutic applications including breast and skin cancer management and have demonstrated promising results with controlled nanoparticle characteristics. The company has also initiated intellectual property protection for the technology, creating a foundation for future development of advanced targeted drug delivery solutions.
Speaking on the occasion, Shri Rajesh Kumar Pathak, Secretary, TDB, stated, "The future of pharmaceutical manufacturing lies in sustainable, technology-driven production systems that combine quality, efficiency, and environmental responsibility. Indigenous development of advanced APIs, critical intermediates, and novel drug delivery platforms will be instrumental in strengthening India's position as a global pharmaceutical manufacturing hub. TDB is committed to supporting such innovative enterprises that are creating high-value technologies while contributing to import substitution and healthcare security."

articles.full_content:
India Continues to Be Among the World's Fastest-Growing Major Economies Despite Global Uncertainties: Shri Jitin Prasada GBRC 2026 Features Directors' Conclaves, Research Deliberations and Special Session on BRICS Amid Geopolitical Turbulence
Minister of State for Commerce and Industry Shri Jitin Prasada today inaugurated the Global Business Research Conference (GBRC) 2026 at the Indian Institute of Foreign Trade (IIFT), a Deemed-to-be University under the Ministry of Commerce and Industry, Government of India. The conference, themed “Managing Business Amidst Worldwide Turbulence”, has brought together eminent academicians, policymakers, management educators, researchers and industry experts from India and abroad to deliberate on emerging challenges and opportunities shaping the global business environment.
Addressing the gathering, Shri Jitin Prasada said that despite prevailing global uncertainties, India continues to remain one of the world's fastest-growing major economies. He noted that under the leadership of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi, the country has adopted a long-term vision focused on strengthening trade, manufacturing, innovation and technological capabilities. India's progress in areas such as artificial intelligence, semiconductors and global trade partnerships, he said, reflects its growing role in the international economy.
The Minister acknowledged the significant contribution of institutions such as IIFT in generating research-driven insights, developing future business leaders and supporting India's integration with global markets. He expressed confidence that the deliberations at GBRC 2026 would generate valuable insights and recommendations that could contribute to policymaking and India's economic growth.
Vice-Chancellor, IIFT, Prof. Rakesh Mohan Joshi said that the institute has expanded its research footprint and international collaborations while strengthening its position as a centre of excellence in international business education and policy research. He noted that the Global Business Research Conference reflects IIFT's commitment to fostering rigorous academic inquiry and facilitating meaningful dialogue among scholars, policymakers and institutional leaders on issues shaping global trade, business competitiveness and economic development. Speaking on the occasion, Prof. Joshi said that the global business landscape is being reshaped by geopolitical developments, evolving trade dynamics and technological disruptions, particularly artificial intelligence. He emphasized that as countries strengthen economic resilience and expand international partnerships, research and evidence-based policy inputs have become increasingly important. ESIC launches centralized online patient feedback system to strengthen patient-centric healthcare delivery
articles.full_content:
ESIC introduces digital feedback platform across all hospitals and dispensaries nationwide
Mumbai, 4 June 2026
The Employees’ State Insurance Corporation (ESIC), under the Ministry of Labour & Employment, has taken a significant step towards strengthening service quality and ensuring patient-centric healthcare delivery by successfully implementing a Centralized Online Patient Feedback System across all its hospitals and dispensaries nationwide. This digital initiative enables Insured Persons (IPs) and beneficiaries to conveniently share their healthcare experiences, report specific concerns, and offer actionable suggestions for institutional improvement. The platform systematically captures citizen feedback on key service parameters, including cleanliness of facilities, behaviour of medical staff, and availability of essential medicines, thereby supporting continuous improvement in service delivery.
Seamless Accessibility and Multilingual Support
To ensure wider participation and ease of access, the system provides multiple feedback channels for beneficiaries. These include automated SMS-based feedback links sent directly to Insured Persons immediately after availing services through the ESIC HIS (Dhanwantri) Module. In addition, on-site QR codes displayed on prominently placed, customized multilingual posters across all OPDs and hospital premises enable quick and convenient submission of feedback. Beneficiaries may also submit their responses through the official ESIC web portal. The user-friendly interface allows patients to simply scan the QR code using their mobile devices, enter their IP number, rate their experience, and submit feedback within seconds.
To ensure the authenticity and integrity of the feedback mechanism, the system incorporates real-time validation of Insured Person (IP) details through a secure OTP-based verification process, along with robust safeguards to prevent duplicate submissions. In addition, the platform offers comprehensive multilingual support, thereby ensuring inclusivity and enabling effective participation from India’s diverse workforce across regions and languages.
Tiered Monitoring and Rapid Grievance Redressal
To translate patient feedback into immediate administrative action, the system integrates robust, role-based digital dashboards. This enables continuous, real-time performance monitoring across three distinct administrative tiers:
• Headquarters Level
• Regional Offices Level
• Local ESI Health Facilities Level
Crucially, the system automatically flags any service rating below 3, triggering prompt alerts to the concerned authorities for timely corrective action. It also enables performance ranking of healthcare facilities, promoting continuous improvement, healthy competition, and stronger institutional accountability.
PIB Mumbai | Sriyanka Chatterjee/Robin Singh/Darshana Rane

SAI NERC IMPHAL ANNOUNCES OPEN SELECTION TRIALS
articles.full_content:
The Sports Authority of India North East Regional Centre (SAI NERC) is officially launching a Nationwide open selection trial to induct exceptional youth football talent into the elite National Centre of Excellence (NCOE) residential program. The comprehensive scouting event will take place at the SAI NERC Football Ground in Takyelpat, Imphal, running from June 6 to June 13, 2026. Designed specifically to elevate dedicated grassroots players to the highest professional tiers of Indian football, interested athletes must download the official registration form online and present it alongside mandatory physical documents, on their designated trial day.
The NCOE scheme is open to boys and girls born between January 1, 2009 and December 31, 2015. The selection trials for boys will be conducted from June 6 to June 9, 2026, while trials for girls will be held from June 10 to June 13, 2026. All participants are required to report at the SAI NERC Football Ground, Takyelpat, at 7:00 AM on their respective trial dates.
During physical verification, all candidates must produce their original Aadhaar Card, original Birth Certificate, four recent passport-size photographs, educational certificates, and National or International Sports Performance Certificates (if applicable).
SAI NERC Football Coach Devender Dhillon emphasized the institution’s commitment and urged youth from every corner of the country to capitalize on this premier developmental ecosystem and test their mettle against the best in their age group.
To participate in the selection process, candidates must download the official registration form from the SAI NERC website at https://sainercimphal.in/downloads . Athletes must submit the duly filled form on the day of trial. During physical verification, all candidates must produce their original Aadhaar Card, original Birth Certificate, four recent passport-size photographs, educational certificates, and National or International Sports Performance Certificates (if applicable).

World Environment Day Special: Green Highways Being Built with Fly Ash and Industrial Waste
articles.full_content:
National Highway Projects Giving New Direction to Environmental Protection and Sustainable Development
Promoting environmental conservation and sustainable development, the use of fly ash and industrial waste materials in national highway construction is being adopted on a large scale across the country. In various projects in Chhattisgarh, green highways are being developed using crores of metric tonnes of fly ash along with alternative materials such as steel slag, waste tyre rubber and bio-bitumen, helping reduce carbon emissions while ensuring scientific disposal of industrial waste.
The National Highways Authority of India is undertaking several innovative initiatives to maintain ecological balance alongside the development of modern road infrastructure. Fly ash generated from thermal power plants is being extensively utilized in highway construction projects. During 2024-25, a record 21.7 million metric tonnes of fly ash was used in various highway projects in Chhattisgarh, while more than 6.2 million metric tonnes was utilized during 2025-26. In the current financial year 2026-27, nearly 2 million metric tonnes of fly ash has already been used in road construction works.
In addition, the recycling and reuse of alternative materials such as steel slag from the steel industry, waste tyre rubber and bio-bitumen are also being actively promoted. A total of 30,477 metric tonnes of such materials was used in green highway construction during 2024-25, while 2,691.29 metric tonnes was utilized in 2025-26. These initiatives have not only strengthened the scientific management of industrial waste but have also made highway construction more environment-friendly.
National highway projects are also prioritizing water conservation and groundwater recharge measures. Rainwater harvesting pits are being developed to strengthen water conservation efforts, while treated water obtained from Sewage Treatment Plants is being utilized for construction activities and plantation irrigation.
Keeping wildlife conservation in focus, eco-friendly infrastructure is being developed in environmentally sensitive forest regions. In Chhattisgarh’s Sitanadi-Udanti sanctuary area, an approximately three-kilometre-long tunnel is being constructed to ensure minimum disturbance to the natural forest ecosystem. Special structures such as elephant passes, animal underpasses and monkey canopies are also being developed to facilitate safe movement of wildlife.
To promote green highways, large-scale plantation drives are also being undertaken along national highways. Under the “Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam 2.0” campaign, more than 2.5 lakh saplings were planted along highways and dividers in Chhattisgarh during the previous year, marking a significant achievement in the direction of green development and environmental conservation.

In various projects in Chhattisgarh, green highways are being developed using crores of metric tonnes of fly ash along with alternative materials such as steel slag, waste tyre rubber and bio-bitumen, helping reduce carbon emissions while ensuring scientific disposal of industrial waste. The National Highways Authority of India is undertaking several innovative initiatives to maintain ecological balance alongside the development of modern road infrastructure.

articles.full_content:
India and the United States issued a Joint Statement on 7 th February 2026 agreeing on a framework for an Interim Agreement regarding reciprocal and mutually beneficial trade. The framework reaffirmed the countries’ commitment to the broader India-U.S. Bilateral Trade Agreement negotiations. A delegation from the Office of the United States Trade Representative (USTR), led by the Chief Negotiator, visited India from 1–4 June 2026 to advance discussions on the Trade Agreement. During the visit, the teams held constructive and positive discussions across a wide range of issues covering Trade in Goods, Non-Tariff Measures, Customs and Trade Facilitation, Economic Security Alignment and other areas of mutual interest.
The engagements were marked by a spirit of cooperation and pragmatism, with both sides reaffirming their commitment to concluding a mutually beneficial agreement that strengthens bilateral trade and economic ties.
Abhijith Narayanan/ Garima Singh/ Ishita Biswas

articles.full_content:
Ministry of Minority Affairs Organises Reforms Utsav at Bharat Mandapam; Signs MoU with EkStep Foundation to Accelerate AI-led Digital Transformation
As part of the nationwide celebrations marking twelve years of transformative governance, Ministry of Minority Affairs today became the first ministry of the Government of India to organise a dedicated “Reforms Utsav” to commemorate twelve years of reform-oriented governance and citizen-centric service delivery.
The event organised at Bharat Mandapam in New Delhi, showcased the Ministry’s reform-driven initiatives aimed at ensuring inclusive development, transparency, ease of access and technology-enabled service delivery for minority communities across the country.
The programme was attended by the Shri Kiren Rijiju, Union Minister of Parliamentary Affairs and Minority Affairs , Shri George Kurian, Union Minister of State for Minority Affairs, Dr. Srivatsa Krishna, Secretary, Ministry of Minority Affairs and senior officers of the Ministry; representatives of all six notified minority communities; and beneficiaries of various schemes of the Ministry from across the country.
Shri Kiren Rijiju in his address highlighted the Government’s unwavering commitment to inclusive growth and empowerment of all sections of society. He stated that technology has emerged as a powerful instrument for improving governance, enhancing transparency and ensuring efficient delivery of welfare schemes. He noted that over the last twelve years, the Ministry has undertaken a series of reforms to make its programmes more accessible, accountable and outcome-oriented.
The Minister reiterated the Government’s vision of building a Viksit Bharat by 2047, where every citizen has access to opportunities, quality education, skills, housing, healthcare, digital connectivity and a dignified livelihood. He said that the experiences shared by beneficiaries during the event reflected the positive impact of various schemes and programmes being implemented by the Ministry.
Shri George Kurian, in his address, emphasised the importance of last-mile delivery and community participation in ensuring that the benefits of government schemes reach every eligible beneficiary. He highlighted the Ministry’s efforts to strengthen outreach and improve citizen engagement through digital platforms and grassroots networks.
Secretary, Ministry of Minority Affairs, Dr. Srivatsa Krishna, outlined the Ministry’s vision for its third term based on the principles of “Reform, Perform, Transform and Inform.” He highlighted the significant progress made in digitisation, skill development, educational empowerment and transparency in service delivery. He stressed the need to leverage emerging technologies, including Artificial Intelligence, to further improve governance outcomes and citizen experience. India & Zimbabwe reaffirm commitment to deepen bilateral defence cooperation during inaugural JDC meeting
articles.full_content:
The inaugural meeting of India-Zimbabwe Joint Defence Committee (JDC) took place in New Delhi on June 04, 2026. Both sides agreed to enhance bilateral defence ties by expanding existing cooperation in areas such as training, support and maintenance of common air assets. They also discussed ways to collaborate in new areas including joint manufacturing, counter-terrorism, border management and peacekeeping.
The meeting was co-chaired by Joint Secretary Shri Amitabh Prasad from the Indian side and Permanent Secretary, Zimbabwe Mr Aaron Daniel Tonde Nhepera, who is leading a 12-member high-level delegation on a two-day visit to India. The Indian delegation comprised representatives from the Services, Department of Defence Production and Armed Forces Medical Services.
India and Zimbabwe share a steadily deepening defence partnership. The first JDC meeting has further reinforced the ties by operationalising the objectives laid down in the MoU on Defence Cooperation signed by the Defence Ministers of both the countries on the sidelines of Aero India 2025.
The delegation from Zimbabwe also visited the DPSU Bhawan in New Delhi, where they discussed potential areas of mutual cooperation with representatives from the Indian defence industry. Mr Aaron Daniel Tonde Nhepera commenced his visit by laying a wreath and paying homage to the fallen heroes at the National War Memorial, New Delhi.

To ensure wider participation and ease of access, the system provides multiple feedback channels for beneficiaries. These include automated SMS-based feedback links sent directly to Insured Persons immediately after availing services through the ESIC HIS (Dhanwantri) Module. In addition, on-site QR codes displayed on prominently placed, customized multilingual posters across all OPDs and hospital premises enable quick and convenient submission of feedback. Beneficiaries may also submit their responses through the official ESIC web portal. The user-friendly interface allows patients to simply scan the QR code using their mobile devices, enter their IP number, rate their experience, and submit feedback within seconds.
To ensure the authenticity and integrity of the feedback mechanism, the system incorporates real-time validation of Insured Person (IP) details through a secure OTP-based verification process, along with robust safeguards to prevent duplicate submissions. In addition, the platform offers comprehensive multilingual support, thereby ensuring inclusivity and enabling effective participation from India’s diverse workforce across regions and languages.
Tiered Monitoring and Rapid Grievance Redressal
To translate patient feedback into immediate administrative action, the system integrates robust, role-based digital dashboards. This enables continuous, real-time performance monitoring across three distinct administrative tiers:
• Headquarters Level
• Regional Offices Level
• Local ESI Health Facilities Level
Crucially, the system automatically flags any service rating below 3, triggering prompt alerts to the concerned authorities for timely corrective action. It also enables performance ranking of healthcare facilities, promoting continuous improvement, healthy competition, and stronger institutional accountability.
PIB Mumbai | Sriyanka Chatterjee/Robin Singh/Darshana Rane

articles.summary: Mumbai, 4 June 2026 The Employees’ State Insurance Corporation (ESIC), under the Ministry of Labour & Employment, has taken a significant step towards strengthening service quality and ensuring patient-centric healthcare delivery by successfully implementing a Centralized Online Patient Feedback System across all its hospitals and dispensaries nationwide. This digital initiative enables Insured Persons (IPs) and beneficiaries to conveniently share their healthcare experiences, report specific concerns, and offer actionable suggestions for institutional improvement. The platform systematically captures citizen feedback on key service parameters, including cleanliness of facilities, behaviour of medical staff, and availability of essential medicines, thereby supporting continuous improvement in service delivery.
